Title: How to Perform the Hover Card Trick The hover card trick is an impressive magic trick that involves making a playing card appear to float or hover above your hand. This trick requires some practice but can be mastered with patience and persistence. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do the hover card trick: ### Materials Needed * A deck of cards (preferably without any large logos) * Clear tape * Scissors **Step 1: Preparation** Take one card from the deck and flip it over so its backside faces upwards. Apply two small pieces of clear tape (about half an inch apart) horizontally across the center of this card as shown below: [Card Diagram] These tapes will serve as hidden grips for your thumb when performing the trick later. Make sure they are placed symmetrically, close enough together yet far enough apart to allow room for your fingernail grip. **Step 2: Setting Up the Grip** Hold the prepared card between your index finger and middle finger at their base knuckles, curving them slightly downward while keeping the rest of your fingers extended outward. Your ring finger and pinky should point straight, helping maintain balance and stability. Position your thumb directly beneath the taped area on the card's backside. **Step 3: Creating Tension** Now curl your thumb forward just enough to feel tension against the tapes without actually touching them. The goal here is to create slight upward lift by using these invisible 'grips'. Remember, less is more; too much force could cause the card to fly off unexpectedly. **Step 4: Concealing the Movement** As you begin lifting the card, make sure to move your wrist backward simultaneously. This motion hides the action behind your hand, preventing spectators from seeing the movement creating the illusion of floating. Practice this until you find a smooth rhythm where the card seems to levitate effortlessly. **Step 5: Perfecting the Angle** Practice holding the card higher or lower depending on what angle works best for hiding the taped section completely. Ideally, it would help if you held it high enough that only the bottom part of the card is visible to viewers. Be mindful of lighting conditions since harsh shadows might reveal the setup. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** * Choose a solid colored background to perform this trick, avoiding patterns or designs that may interfere with visual perception. * Always keep practicing! It takes time to build muscle memory and coordination needed to pull off the trick smoothly. * Start slow and gradually increase speed once comfortable with the technique. Rushing through the steps often leads to mistakes. * Engage with your audience during performance. Distraction plays a crucial role in successful magic tricks. * If possible, wear sleeves long enough to cover most of your forearm to prevent revealing the preparation process. Remember, like all magic tricks, the real secret lies in presentation and showmanship. So don't forget to add flair and excitement to your act! XXX get into all the dust under there. This maths worksheet for year 2 children looks at pairs of numbers which make 20. Before doing this children should be confident with, and know by heart, all pairs of numbers which make 10. Once they know these, pairs that make 20 becomes easy; just one of each pair is ten more e.g. 6 and 4: 16 and 4. Being able to work quickly with mental arithmetic is one of the keys to success in school as so much of the maths lesson today is carried out orally and children gain or lose confidence very quickly when answering in front of a whole class. - Free Year 2 Maths Worksheets - 2009 Maths Blog [GAR manipulates some of the objects on the table] Let's make another piece of paper alright? Sometimes they kicked it too hard and it went over the fence. Then they had to ask the neighbor, Mr. Brown, to give it back. One day, they saw a shiny thing on the ground. It was a screw. Lily picked it up and showed it to Max. "Look, a screw!" she said. "What is it for?" "I don't know," Max said. "Maybe it fell from a car or a bike. Let's keep it. It's cool." They put the screw in their pocket and went back to kicking the ball. They did not see Mr. Brown watching them from his window. He looked angry. He had been fixing his lawnmower and he needed that screw. The next time they kicked the ball over the fence, they ran to the gate and called Mr. Brown. "Mr. Brown, can we have our ball back, please?" they asked. Mr. Brown came out with the ball in his hand. He looked at them and said, "Sure, you can have your ball back. But first, you have to give me something back. Something that belongs to me. Something that you took from my yard." Lily and Max looked at each other. They did not know what he meant. They had not taken anything from his yard. They only played with their ball. They felt scared. "What do you want, Mr. Brown?" Lily asked. Mr. Brown pointed at their pocket. "I want my screw back. The one you found on the ground. The one that I need to fix my lawnmower. The one that you stole from me." Lily and Max gasped. They remembered the screw. They did not know it was his. They did not mean to steal it. They took it out of their pocket and gave it to him. "We're sorry, Mr. Brown," they said. "We didn't know it was yours. We thought it was just a regular screw. We won't take anything from your yard again." Mr. Brown took the screw and nodded. He gave them their ball back. "Thank you, kids," he said. "Now you know. Don't take things that are not yours. And don't kick the ball so hard. It might break something." Lily and Max nodded. They said thank you and ran back to their yard. They decided to play something else. They did not want to make Mr. Brown angry again. They learned their lesson. What does a computer's hard disk have in common with a tall oak? While it may not look like a tree on the outside, the hard disk is organized the same way. Directories of files and folders on a hard drive are organized into branches, where each directory is a branch with files and folders. Folders make up the branches, while files are the leaves. Therefore a leaf is a file within a directory on your hard drive. Technically speaking, a leaf is a node on a tree with no child nodes. Because files cannot have child nodes like folders can, they are always leafs. When referring to a tree structure, a leaf can also be called a leaf node. Title: How to Divide a Large Room Introduction: Dividing a large room can help create separate functional spaces, improve organization, and enhance d cor. This task may seem daunting at first, but with careful planning and execution, you can successfully divide your space while maintaining an open and airy feel. Here's our comprehensive guide on dividing a large room, complete with detailed steps, helpful tips, and crucial guidelines. Step 1: Analyze Your Needs Determine the primary purposes of the divided areas. For example, one section could be used as a living area, another for dining or home office setup. Consider traffic flow, lighting requirements, privacy needs, and storage solutions during this process. By identifying these factors early, you ensure that the final result meets all your expectations and serves its intended functions well. Key Tip: Keep proportions in mind; ideally, each resulting space should occupy about half of the total floor area. However, adjustments might be necessary depending on specific needs. Step 2: Choose Appropriate Room Dividers Select a divider based on aesthetics, functionality, and budget. Some popular options include: * Bookshelves: They provide both visual interest and extra storage. Opt for sturdy, freestanding models if possible. * Curtains or drapes: These are versatile, cost-effective, and easy to install. Use decorative rods and rings for added appeal. * Sliding doors: Offer flexibility, especially when soundproofing is required. * Folding screens: Provide instant division and come in various styles and materials. * Wall partitions: Permanent solution offering excellent noise reduction and privacy. Guideline: Always prioritize safety. Avoid heavy or unstable dividers in high-traffic zones or around children and pets. Step 3: Measure Carefully Accurately measure the width, length, and height of the room, then mark where the divider will stand. Remember to allow enough clearance for any door swings or passageways. Also, consider whether electrical outlets, light fixtures, or heating vents need access after installation. Key Tip: Mark measurements using painter's tape - it won't damage walls or floors and can be removed once work is done. Step 4: Installation Follow manufacturer instructions closely when setting up purchased dividers such as bookshelves, curtains, sliding doors, or folding screens. If building custom wall partitions, consult professional contractors or carpenters for safe and efficient construction. Guideline: Double-check alignment before securing the divider permanently. Misaligned structures can lead to uneven divisions and potential hazards. Step 5: Decorate Thoughtfully Decorating each segment thoughtfully contributes significantly to achieving the desired atmosphere. Match color schemes, incorporate similar design elements, and utilize identical (or complementary) furniture pieces within each section. Balancing consistency with individual character creates cohesive yet distinct spaces. Key Tips: * Layer rugs to define boundaries between sections. * Hang mirrors strategically to reflect natural light and make smaller areas appear larger. * Utilize multi-functional furnishings to maximize utility without cluttering the space. By following these five steps, along with their respective tips and guidelines, you'll have effectively divided your large room into comfortable, stylish, and purposeful segments tailored to meet your lifestyle demands. Happy decorating! When you use all the bubble bath. oh, what did you eat for lunch? In the late 17th century, Robert Boyle found that air is needed for combustion. English chemist John Mayow added to this by showing that fire only needed a part of air. We now call this oxygen (O). He found that a candle burning in a closed container made the water rise to replace a fourteenth of the air's volume before it went out. The same thing happened when a live mouse was put into the box. From this, he worked out that oxygen is used for both respiration and combustion. In 1846, as the Mexican-American War was starting, some Americans in California hoped to create a California Republic. These men flew a "Bear flag" that had a golden bear with a star on it. This Republic ended suddenly, however, when Commodore John D. Sloat of the United States Navy sailed into San Francisco Bay. He said that California was now part of the United States. After the war with Mexico ended, California was split between the two countries. The Mexican portion became the Mexican states of Baja California Norte (north) and Baja California Sur (south). [throws ball, ball rolls out of camera view] Designing your own bookmarks can be a fun and creative way to add a personal touch to your reading experience. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to design your own bookmarks: **Step 1: Gather Supplies** The first step in designing your own bookmark is to gather all the necessary supplies. You will need the following: * Paper or cardstock (preferably heavyweight) * Ruler * Pencil * Scissors or paper cutter * Glue stick or double-sided tape * Decorative elements such as stickers, markers, colored pencils, ribbons, etc. Key tip: Choose sturdy materials that won't tear easily when you slide them into your books. Cardstock is an excellent choice because it is both durable and easy to decorate. **Step 2: Measure and Cut** Decide how large you want your bookmark to be. A standard size is about 2 inches by 6 inches, but feel free to adjust this based on your preference. Using your ruler and pencil, measure and mark the desired dimensions onto your paper or cardstock. Then carefully cut along the lines using scissors or a paper cutter. Guideline: Keep in mind that larger bookmarks may not fit well in some books, so consider testing different sizes before committing to one. **Step 3: Create a Hole** To make your bookmark functional, create a hole at the top where you can attach a tassel, ribbon, or keyring. Use a hole punch if available; otherwise, poke a small hole with a sharp object like a needle or awl. Make sure the hole is big enough for whatever attachment method you choose. Key tip: If working with children, ensure adult supervision during this step due to potential safety risks associated with sharps. **Step 4: Attach Decorative Elements** Now comes the fun part - decorating! There are countless ways to customize your bookmark. Some ideas include: * Drawing illustrations or patterns with markers or colored pencils * Adding stickers or washi tape * Gluing on fabric scraps, buttons, or other embellishments * Writing inspirational quotes or motivational phrases Remember to leave space near the bottom edge for holding the page. Also, avoid adding too many three-dimensional elements that could interfere with closing the book. **Step 5: Secure Edges** After decorating, seal the edges of your bookmark to prevent fraying and increase durability. Apply glue stick or double-sided tape along the sides and/or top and bottom edges, then press down firmly. Allow any adhesive to dry completely before moving on to the next step. Guideline: When applying adhesive, be careful not to let it seep through to the front side of your bookmark, especially if you used light colors that might show smudges. **Step 6: Add an Attachment** Finally, thread your chosen attachment material (ribbon, cord, etc.) through the hole created earlier and secure it tightly. Tie a knot or bow at the end to keep it from slipping out. Alternatively, you can attach a keyring or split ring instead. And there you have it - your very own unique bookmark! With these steps, you now possess the knowledge to create endless variations of personalized bookmarks tailored to your taste or as gifts for others. Happy crafting! He was too excited and ran to the kite to grab it. Suddenly, a tough foot stepped on the kite. John was scared and turned around. There was a big man with a mean look on his face. Mommy, John said, scared. The man said, I run the market and I won t provide for any children running around and taking things that don t belong to them. John was still scared and backed away slowly. His mom told the man she was sorry and would pay for the kite. But the man said it was too late and he wanted John to learn a lesson. This transformation requires careful planning, creativity, and attention to detail. By following our step-by-step instructions, you'll successfully recreate Marty McFify's look while engaging with other users in this fun online community. Section 1: Choosing Your Penguin Character Step 1: Create a New Account or Log In If you don't already have an account, sign up for one at the Club Penguin website. If you do, simply log in using your credentials. Make sure that you are logged into the correct server before proceeding. Key Tip: Choose a penguin name reminiscent of Marty McFly, such as 'MartyPeng' or 'McFlyPeng.' Avoid names containing profanities or offensive language. Step 2: Customize Your Penguin Avatar Upon logging in, you will be prompted to customize your penguin avatar. You can change its appearance later; however, doing so now ensures consistency throughout the process. Here is what you need to create a Marty McFly inspired penguin: * Head: Default head (no special additions) * Body: Red jacket resembling the one worn by Marty McFly * Feet: Regular black boots or sneakers similar to those worn by Marty * Background: Any background related to time travel or the 80s would work well Guidelines: * Use color codes #9b0000 for red and #000000 for black when selecting clothing colors. These approximate the original colors used in the movies. * Remember that exact matches may not always be possible due to limited options available on Club Penguin. Aim for a close representation instead. Section 2: Gathering Props and Accessories Step 3: Obtain Necessary Items To further enhance your Marty McFly persona, gather these items through various methods such as purchasing them via the game's store or trading with fellow players: * Guitar: Symbolizes Marty's passion for music and his performances during the films. * Skateboard: Represents Marty's love for skating and serves as another visual cue connecting him to the movies. Step 4: Equip Your Items Once acquired, equip your guitar and skateboard by accessing your inventory and dragging them onto your penguin. Now they appear whenever you interact with others or move around the map. Section 3: Engaging With Other Players Step 5: Interact With Fellow Users Now that you have completed transforming yourself into Marty McFly, engage with other players by starting conversations about topics relevant to the movies or sharing stories about adventures involving your new identity. Step 6: Participate in Relevant Activities Join clubs or groups centered around themes like music, time traveling, or science fiction. Attend events where dressing up as favorite characters is encouraged and showcase your Marty McFly attire proudly! Conclusion: By meticulously following these steps, you can effectively become Marty McFly within the vibrant universe of Club Penguin. Enjoy roleplaying as this beloved cinematic figure while forming connections with like-minded individuals who share your interests and enthusiasm. Title: How to Heal Lips After Biting Them Introduction: Bitting your lips can cause discomfort, pain, swelling, and even bleeding. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to heal your lips after biting them. By following these steps, you'll be able to soothe the irritation, promote healing, and prevent future incidents. Step 1: Cleanse the Bitten Area Rinse your mouth with warm water to clean the affected area gently. Avoid using toothpaste or mouthwash containing alcohol as they may aggravate the wound. If necessary, use a mild soap and water solution to clean the outer part of your lip. Make sure to rinse thoroughly and pat dry with a soft towel. Key Tip: Keeping the wounded area clean is essential to preventing infection and promoting quicker healing. Step 2: Apply a Cold Compress Apply a cold compress or an ice pack wrapped in a cloth to the bitten area for about 10-15 minutes at a time, several times per day. This process reduces inflammation and numbs the area, which alleviates pain and minimizes swelling. Guideline: Never place ice directly onto the skin; always wrap it in a cloth or use a cold compress to avoid frostbite. Step 3: Moisturize Your Lips Use a petroleum jelly or a lip balm without fragrance or color to keep your lips moisturized throughout the healing process. These products create a protective barrier that prevents moisture loss, promotes healing, and relieves pain caused by chapping or cracking. Key Tip: Choose a product specifically designed for sensitive skin or lips to minimize potential allergic reactions. Step 4: Stay Hydrated Drink plenty of fluids, especially water, to maintain proper hydration levels both internally and externally. Proper hydration facilitates faster recovery and keeps your skin supple, reducing the likelihood of further damage due to dryness. Guideline: Aim for eight glasses (64 oz) of water daily but adjust based on individual needs and activity level. Step 5: Use Over-the-Counter Pain Relievers If needed, take over-the-counter pain medications such as ibuprofen or acetaminophen to help manage any lingering discomfort from the bite. Follow package directions carefully regarding dosage and frequency. Key Tip: Consult a healthcare professional if you have questions about taking medication or are experiencing severe symptoms. Step 6: Prevent Further Irritation Avoid picking or peeling scabs formed during the healing process, as this delays tissue repair and increases the risk of infection. Refrain from licking, biting, or scratching your lips, and wear lip sunscreen when going outdoors to protect against UV radiation. Guideline: Consider wearing a lip guard or dental wax to shield the injured area from additional trauma while sleeping or engaging in activities where accidental contact might occur. Conclusion: By following these steps and adhering to the provided guidelines, you should experience noticeable improvement within a few days. Remember, patience and diligent care are crucial elements in ensuring optimal healing. Should symptoms worsen or persist beyond one week, consult a medical professional for further evaluation and treatment options. I think that these guys should get in a boat and go on a trip. We are Siamese if you please right? Water on Mars In many ways, Mars is the most Earthlike of all the planets. A visitor would find that a day on Mars is only slightly longer than one on Earth. Mars is also tilted about the same amount as the Earth, so it has seasons. Both Mars and Earth have white polar caps. Those on Mars are much smaller and thinner, so they grow quickly in winter and almost disappear in summer. They are much colder than the polar caps on Earth, so they contain frozen carbon dioxide (the 'dry ice' used to create fog in stage shows) as well as water ice. Mars is in the middle of an ice age, so liquid water cannot exist on its surface at the present time. However, the planet seems to have been warmer and wetter in the past. Winding channels that look like dry river beds have been seen in spacecraft images. They suggest that huge amounts of water once flowed over the surface - possibly fed by melting ice. There may even have been rain and snow. Many of the channels seem to have filled large craters or emptied onto the northern plains. Some scientists believe there was a large ocean that covered the northern half of Mars. Stations may be operated as Load following power plant, peaking power plant, or base load power plant. Title: How to Dress Like a Ninja Introduction: Ninjas were covert agents or mercenaries in feudal Japan who specialized in unorthodox warfare. A significant part of their training involved stealth and camouflage, which included their clothing. This article will guide you through the process of dressing like a ninja, providing an authentic look while keeping functionality and comfort in mind. Step 1: Choose the Right Fabric Materials: Black cotton fabric (preferably lightweight) Explanation: Authentic ninja outfits were typically made from dark colored, natural fabrics that blended well with nighttime surroundings. Cotton is breathable, comfortable, and easy to find. The color black is traditionally associated with ninjas, but dark blue or grey can also work effectively depending on your environment. Key Tips: - Opt for matte finish materials as shiny surfaces may reflect light making you visible. - Avoid loud patterns or bright colors. Step 2: Cutting and Sewing the Clothes Materials: Scissors, sewing machine or needle and thread Explanation: Ninja suits are loose-fitting and consist of two main parts - pants and a jacket called a shirt ('kataginu') tied together by a sash ('obi'). To make these, cut out two sets of identical pattern pieces for both tops and bottoms. Remember, straight lines are easier to manage than curves when starting out. Then stitch them together along the sides leaving room only at the armpit area for movement. Guidelines: - For the top, create a 'T' shape template. Make sure it's large enough to allow free movement. - For the pants, design a simple rectangular shape with wide legs. Again, ensure there's ample space for mobility. Step 3: Creating the Obi Materials: Long strip of cloth (same material as the suit), safety pins Explanation: The obi serves dual purposes; holding the kataginu and hakama together and hiding any modern accessories underneath. It should be long enough to wrap around your waist twice. Secure it using safe pins to avoid injury during movements. Step 4: Adding Accessories Materials: Ankle and wrist guards, face mask, sword (optional), sandals Explanation: Ankle and wrist guards provide extra protection during combat situations. Face masks cover most of the head except the eyes, helping maintain anonymity. If you want to carry a weapon, choose a small replica sword. Traditional wooden sandals (geta) complete the outfit. Step 5: Testing Your Outfit Activity: Perform various motions such as running, jumping, crouching, etc. Explanation: Your ninja costume must never restrict your movements. After completing your attire, test its flexibility by performing different actions. Adjust where necessary until you reach optimal comfort level. Conclusion: While this tutorial provides a basic guideline on how to dress like a ninja, remember that true mastery comes from consistent practice and understanding the philosophy behind the art. Always prioritize safety, respect cultural significance, and enjoy exploring the world of the mysterious shadow warriors! But yeah we don't have any food items at home. In many problems, we often divide a large space into smaller areas. For instance, a house is divided into rooms, a nation into states, a type of quantity into numbers, and so on. Each of these smaller areas (room, state, number) is next to other small areas (other rooms/states/numbers). The places where the areas meet are connections. If we write down on paper a list of spaces, and the connections between them, we have written down a description of a space -- a topological space. All topological spaces have the same properties such as connections, and are made of the same structure (a list of smaller areas). This makes it easier to study how spaces behave. It also makes it easier to write algorithms. For instance, to program a robot to navigate a house, we simply give it a list of rooms, the connections between each room (doors), and an algorithm that can work out which rooms to go through to reach any other room. For more examples of this type of problem, look at Graph theory. How will we know if we don't try asked Raggedy Ann. The first pope who traveled a lot was Pope Paul VI. I'm guest posting for Heidi so she can take a well deserved vacation! Common Core State Standard 2.OA.2 Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies. By end of Grade 2, know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers. Yes, that says "from memory"! So much of future math depends on knowing these basic facts, and it's not easy for the kids to memorize them. I like to use a lot of games to practice basic addition and subtraction facts. In fact, here are a few games that can be played with a regular deck of cards. Playing cards have many advantages: Using cards frequently increases a child's number sense. They're easy to get, and you don't actually need a complete deck! (Insert joke here about "not playing with a full deck"!) If you ask the kids to bring in spare card decks, you'll have enough to keep the class busy in a couple of days! Addition War: This works almost like regular War, but each player is given two cards. (All picture cards are worth 10 in this game.) The player adds his/ her cards together and says the whole equation. (Brain research tells us that using the voice to verbalize the whole thought helps the memory!) Whoever has the greatest sum wins that round. In the example below, the player on the left would say "8 plus 10 equals 18" and the player on the right would say "5 plus 10 equals 15". The player on the left would win all 4 cards. In this example, the player on the left would say "4 plus 3 equals 7" and the player on the right would say "3 plus 10 equals 13". The player on the right would win all 4 cards. - Use 3 cards. The greatest sum wins each contest. - Use 2 cards to make a 2 digit number. The greatest 2 digit number wins each contest. This also works for 3 digit numbers. - Do subtraction. The smaller number will be subtracted from the larger number. The greatest difference wins each contest. - Each player chooses 3 cards. They make a 2 digit number with 2, then subtract the third. The greatest difference wins each contest. - Take out all the tens and picture cards, and just focus on the single digits, as in the above standard. - Just use the cards you want to focus on... if you're working on the easier facts, remove all cards over five. If you're working on tougher facts, remove the lower numbers. Here's another game, it's called Fishing for 10s: Since we work with a base ten system, forming tens is pretty important. In this game, the picture cards are worth ten. Everything else is face value. To start the game, each player gets 5 cards, and the rest are kept face down in the deck. Before starting, players look at their cards and take out their tens. The player on the left took out the 6 and 4. The player on the right took out a King and a Jack, which are each worth ten. Then the "fishing" begins. Instead of asking for a match, in "Fishing for Tens", the player must ask for the card that would complete the set of 10. In the picture below, the player on the left had a 3, so asked for a 7. Play continues just as "regular fish". If a player runs out of cards, they can take one from the pile. The game ends when all the cards are gone. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins. This last game is a solitaire game, called Pyramid Solitaire. In this game, we're adding to 13. The Jack is worth 11, the Queen is worth 12, and the King is 13 by itself. To start the game, set up a pyramid, increasing each row by one card, until you have a row of 7 cards across the bottom of the pyramid. The goal of this game is to collect combinations of 13, until all the cards are discarded. This game is very difficult to win, but the kids love the challenge, and will play it over and over. (Which means they're practicing their addition!) Now turn over the first card from the deck. It's a 3. We would have to have a 10 available to use it, so we leave it in the pile. Now we drew another 8, which will go with either 5. This player took the 5 of spades on the corner. These go in the discard pile. It goes with the 6. Now we've opened up the 8 of spades, which goes with the 5 of diamonds, opening up a few more cards! Cards that aren't used as a combination of 13 are kept in order and can be reused when the deck is exhausted. The player keeps turning cards from the deck and discarding combinations of 13 until no more moves are available. As I mentioned above, this game is a popular one! I never mind if the children want to play this as a team. That's when the great conversations happen! (If we use this 8, we'll open up this 6, which goes with this 7!) Brain research tells us conversation is an important part of learning! I thought I'd leave you with a little freebie: the children love these lists because they're set up in patterns. These are all the addition facts and all the subtraction facts that fit the Common Core State Standard 2.OA.2 (Single digit plus single digit.) Just click the image to download the freebie! The YYY got out and flew away. yeah, it's green and beige for the flowers. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV7/LW/trilw7.cha = = = Title: How to Eat a Plum Introduction: Plums are juicy and delicious fruits that can be enjoyed as a snack or used in various recipes. This tutorial will guide you through the steps of eating a plum, providing helpful tips along the way to ensure an enjoyable experience. Things You'll Need: * A ripe plum * Napkin or paper towel (optional) Step 1: Selecting Your Plum Choose a fresh, ripe plum from your local grocery store or farmers market. Look for one with smooth skin free from blemishes, bruises, or cuts. Gently squeeze the fruit; it should have some give but not feel mushy. The color of a ripened plum varies depending on its variety - they come in shades of red, purple, blue, green, and yellow. Step 2: Rinse the Plum Rinse the plum under cool running water to remove any dirt, residue, or pesticides. Pat it dry with a clean cloth or allow it to air-dry before proceeding to the next step. Key Tip: Thoroughly drying the plum makes it easier to handle and reduces the risk of slipping while cutting or biting into it. Step 3: Prepare a Clean Cutting Surface Place the dried plum onto a clean cutting board or plate. Make sure the surface is stable and won't slide around during preparation. Step 4: Slice Around the Seam Locate the natural seam where the two halves of the plum join together. Carefully insert a paring knife along this line, slicing all the way around until you reach the starting point. Do not cut through the stem end. Guideline: Be cautious when using knives, keeping fingers away from blade edges at all times. Step 5: Twist Apart the Halves Hold both ends of the plum firmly and gently twist them apart. They should separate easily along the slice made in Step 4. If necessary, use the tip of the knife to help loosen any stubborn areas. Step 6: Remove the Pit Inspect each half for the pit - it may be loose or still attached to the flesh. To remove the pit, simply lift it out with your fingers or use the tip of the knife to dislodge it if needed. Discard the pit properly. Step 7: Optional Score Cuts To make eating easier, especially for children or those with dental issues, score the flesh of each half by making shallow crisscross cuts without breaking through the skin. This creates small segments that can be easily removed from the stone. Step 8: Enjoy Your Plum You now have two ready-to-eat halves of plum! Pick up one half and bite directly into it like an apple, or use a fork to spear individual pieces. For messier varieties, consider placing a napkin or paper towel underneath to catch juice drips. Key Tips: * Always wash your hands before handling food items. * Store unripe plums at room temperature until they soften slightly, then transfer them to the refrigerator to prolong their freshness. * Consume ripe plums within a few days of purchase to enjoy optimal flavor and texture. Mass is the amount of matter there is in some body. If you knew the number of protons and neutrons in a piece of matter such as a brick, then you could calculate its total mass as the sum of the masses of all the protons and of all the neutrons. (Electrons are so small that they are almost negligible.) Masses pull on each other, and a very large mass such as that of the Earth pulls very hard on things nearby. You would weigh much more on Jupiter than on Earth because Jupiter is so huge. You would weigh much less on the Moon because it is only about one-sixth the mass of Earth. Weight is related to the mass of the brick (or the person) and the mass of whatever is pulling it down on a spring scale - which may be smaller than the smallest moon in the solar system or larger than the Sun. In computer science, a variable is a value in a program that can change. It does not have to be a number. In fact, it can be a string (text value), a date, an amount of money, an object such as a picture, or simply (which means it has no content). The value that is stored in a variable can change the behavior of a program when it is run. Because of this, variables are commonly used to store input and output values. SIGNS AND SYMBOLS OF MUSIC Shared by: RegineBilbao SIGNS AND SYMBOLS OF MUSIC ACCIDENTALS - signs used in music to indicate the raising or lowering of a tone. Accidentals are place before a particular note. 1. Sharp - # - raised a note by a half step (semitone). 2. Flat - - lowers note by a half step. 3. Natural - - cancels the effect of # or 4. Double Sharp - ## - raises the pitch of a note by two semitones or one whole tone. 5. Double Flat - - lowers the pitch of a note by two semitones or one whole tone. NOTES AND REST Note - a term used both to describe a given pitch and the sign used to indicate the value of a note. Rest - a sign which denotes silence or pause. For example, if it takes two beats to sing a whole note. The corresponding whole rest will also have two beats. WHOLE HALF QUARTER EIGHT 16TH 32ND 64TH NOTE REST SIGNS AND SYMBOLS OF MUSIC a. Staff - a set of 5 horizontal lines and spaces between them on which notes can be place to show the pitches of musical tones. b. Ledger Lines - added lines below or above the staff to accommodate notes that are beyond the staff. c. Bar-line - a vertical line drawn through the musical staff. _____________________----__________ __________________________________ __________________________________ __________________________________ __________________________________ d. The Pitch of musical sounds is shown by little oval characteristics, called notes, on lines and in spaces; these notes are named after the 7 letter/s of the alphabet, A, B, C, D, E, F, G. Some read the notes as solfa syllables: do, re, mi, fa, so, la, ti, (si). See they don't put the same colors together I don't think. On Wednesday, he ate three plums, but he was still hungry. Have you ever tried to estimate the number of jellybeans in a jar? If so, you may have looked at the size of a single jellybean and compared it to the size of the jar. Then, based on size, you may have tried to imagine how many jellybeans could fit in that jar. Or maybe youve tried to estimate how many steps it takes to walk to school. You may not have counted every step. Instead, you may have thought about how many steps it takes you to walk one block; then multiplied that number by the number of blocks you have to walk to get to school. You get the idea. Title: How to Achieve Ariana Grande's Half Up Half Down Hairstyle =============================================================== Ariana Grande is known for her signature half up half down hairstyle. This sleek and stylish look can be achieved with some patience and practice. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to get this iconic hairstyle. Things You'll Need ------------------ 1. Hairbrush 2. Rat-tail comb 3. Hair ties or hair bands 4. Bobby pins 5. Hair spray 6. Heat protectant (optional) 7. Curling iron or wand (optional) 8. Hair serum or oil (optional) ### Step 1: Brushing Your Hair Brush your hair thoroughly using a hairbrush to remove any tangles or knots. This will make the following steps easier and ensure that your hair looks smooth and polished. ### Step 2: Sectioning Off the Top Half of Your Hair In 1998, a study was done in 27 countries. Does she usually play with the baby alone? Lacrosse is a team sport that uses a rubber ball and a lacrosse stick, a racket with a long handle. The ball is about the same size as a standard baseball.In the past, the sticks are were of wood, but modern versions use metal alloys. The lacrosse stick is made up of three parts: - the head - located at the end of the stick and used to control the ball; - the mesh (a.k.a. springing) - the net inside the head that can be used to hold the ball; and - the shaft - the long handle of the stick, which the player holds. Lacrosse is a full contact sport, meaning the players are allowed to engage in body contact as part of the defensive or offensive moves. Two teams with ten people each play against each other. Each team is made up of one goalie, three defensemen, three middies, and three attackmen. A score is gained if a ball is shot from the lacrosse stick to the goal net of the opposing team. Each game consists of four quarters. The team with the most points at the end of the game wins. Members of the other team can take the ball away from you by hitting you with their bodies or their sticks. You can get past the other team's defensemen by doing several types of dodges. There are three basic moves used in the game: - Cradling is practiced so that players won't lose the ball while going against other players. This is done by placing your dominant hand on the part of the shaft closest to the head of the stick. Use your other hand to guide your stick by placing it on the butt of the shaft. Move forward while keeping the lacrosse stick steady. The goal of this is to keep moving while keeping the ball in the net. - Passing allows players in the same team to transfer the balls from one another. Place your dominant hand on the middle of the shaft and the other on the end of the stick. Push your dominant hand away while pulling the other hand towards your body. This will cause the ball to jump from inside the netting and launch towards another teammate. - Once the ball has been launched, another player has to catch it. To catch a ball, put your dominant hand near the head of the stick and the other near its butt. Position the netting where it will catch the ball. Once the ball is in the netting, pull the stick an inch or two toward you to prevent it from bouncing out of the stick. Filed Under: Games About the Author: You knocked all that down like that. Title: How to Back Up a Windows Computer Backup is an essential task that ensures the safety and accessibility of your important files and data even when something unexpected happens to your computer. This tutorial will guide you through backing up your Windows computer using built-in tools like File History and Backup and Restore (Windows 7). By following these steps, you can secure your documents, pictures, music, emails, and other personal files. ### Section 1: Using File History File History is a backup feature introduced in Windows 8 and continued in later versions. It allows users to automatically back up their files to an external drive or network location. Here's how to set it up: **Step 1:** Connect an External Drive Plug in an external hard drive with sufficient storage space for your backups. Make sure it has enough capacity for future backups as well. _Key tip:_ Use a reliable and high-quality external drive for better performance and longevity. **Step 2:** Access File History Settings Open the Start menu and search for 'Control Panel'. Once open, type 'File History' into the search bar at the top right corner and click on 'File History'. **Step 3:** Configure File History Click on 'Select Drive' on the left sidebar and choose the connected external drive from the list. Then, return to the main window and ensure 'Automatically back up my files' is turned ON. **Step 4:** Customize Backup Settings (Optional) You may customize what folders are backed up by clicking on 'Exclude Folders', adding specific directories if necessary. You can also adjust how often backups occur by selecting 'Advanced settings' under the left sidebar. _Guideline:_ Schedule frequent backups (e.g., every hour) for critical files that change frequently. ### Section 2: Using Backup and Restore (Windows 7) This tool is available on all modern Windows systems, offering more advanced options than File History. Follow these steps to create a system image: **Step 1:** Open Backup and Restore (Windows 7) Press Win + X keys simultaneously and select 'Control Panel' > 'System and Security' > 'Backup and Restore (Windows 7)'. **Step 2:** Create System Image On the left pane, click on 'Create a system image' and then confirm administrator permission. **Step 3:** Choose Backup Location Decide where you want to store this image - either on an external drive, DVDs, or another partition of your internal drive. Click 'Next' after making your selection. **Step 4:** Confirm Settings & Begin Backup Review your choices regarding which drives to include in the backup. If everything looks correct, hit 'Start backup'. _Key Tip:_ Keep multiple copies of system images at different points in time so you have various restore options. By completing both sections above, you now have a robust backup strategy for your Windows machine, ensuring peace of mind against potential data loss scenarios. Remember to regularly check and maintain your backups, updating them whenever significant changes occur on your PC. We just pretend to get it off. It's a read this to me. The basket is full of shredded colored paper.] Title: Get Up When You Want With Only A Few Hours Of Sleep Introduction: Sleeping less than the recommended 7-9 hours per night can take a toll on your physical health and mental well-being. However, there are situations when you may need to wake up early despite having slept fewer hours. This guide will provide you with evidence-based steps and strategies to help you get up when you want, even if you've had limited sleep. Keep in mind that this is not a substitute for healthy sleeping habits; rather, consider it as a short-term solution for occasional mornings when you must rise early. Section 1: Preparation (The Night Before) Step 1: Plan Your Schedule * Explanation: Prioritize tasks and allocate time wisely to maximize productivity during the day. By doing so, you create a sense of urgency that encourages you to make the most out of your few hours of sleep. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Identify essential activities and delegate or postpone non-essential ones. + Consider using tools like calendars, planners, or apps to organize your schedule. Step 2: Optimize Your Sleep Environment * Explanation: Create an environment conducive to sleep by controlling factors such as light, noise, temperature, and bedding. An optimal sleep setting facilitates better rest, making it easier to wake up when needed. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Use blackout curtains or wear eye masks to block light. + Utilize earplugs, white noise machines, or fans to mask disruptive sounds. + Set room temperatures between 60-67 F (15-19 C). + Invest in comfortable bedding and pillows. Step 3: Limit Light Exposure Before Bedtime * Explanation: Reducing exposure to artificial blue light emitted from electronic devices before going to bed promotes melatonin production, which regulates sleep-wake cycles. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Stop using electronics at least one hour before bedtime. + Install blue light filters on screens or wear glasses designed to block blue light. + Replace evening screen time with reading a book or listening to relaxing music. Section 2: The Morning Routine Step 4: Gradually Increase Natural Light Exposure * Explanation: As soon as you wake up, expose yourself to natural sunlight to suppress melatonin levels and promote alertness. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Open window blinds or curtains immediately upon rising. + Step outside for a brief walk or perform morning exercises near a sunlit area. + Avoid wearing sunglasses during the first hour after waking up. Step 5: Perform Physical Activities * Explanation: Engaging in physical activity increases heart rate and blood circulation, helping to awaken your body and mind. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Perform a quick workout routine, yoga poses, or jumping jacks. Adding clips in extensions is a great way to add length, volume, or color to your hair without causing damage or committing to a permanent change. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to properly add clips in extensions, ensuring that they are securely attached and blend naturally with your own hair. **Things You'll Need:** 1. Hair extensions with clips (make sure to choose high-quality human hair or synthetic fibers) 2. Rat tail comb 3. Hair sectioning clips 4. Hair brush or wide-tooth comb 5. Mirror 6. Hair spray or other styling products (optional) ### **Step 1: Choosing the Right Extensions** Before purchasing clip-in extensions, consider factors such as texture, color, length, and weight. Matching these aspects closely to your natural hair ensures a more seamless appearance. Pay attention to the number of clips included, their size, and placement along the wefts. More clips offer better security but may require additional time for application. ### **Step 2: Preparation** Brush your hair thoroughly before adding clip-in extensions. Detangle any knots using a hairbrush or wide-tooth comb to prevent pulling out strands when attaching the clips. Divide your hair into sections using hair sectioning clips; this makes it easier to attach the extensions evenly throughout your head. ### **Step 3: Sectioning Your Hair** Create a horizontal part across your head from ear to ear at the crown area where you want to place the first set of clips. Secure the top half of your hair with a hair sectioning clip. Leave the bottom half down, which serves as an anchor for the extension piece. ### **Step 4: Attaching Wefted Extensions** Take one of the wefted extension pieces, open its clips, and position it close to your scalp near the base of the sectioned hair. Make sure there is no gap between your natural hair and the extension. Close the clips firmly by pressing them shut. Be cautious not to overtighten as this can cause discomfort or breakage. Repeat this process for all remaining clips on that particular extension piece. ### **Step 5: Blending Extensions With Natural Hair** After securing the first row of clips, release another small section of unclipped hair directly above the newly attached extension. Gently backcomb this new section using a rat tail comb to create some teasing at the root. Place the next extension piece beneath this teased portion, sandwiching the natural hair between the two layers. Press the clips closed to secure the second layer. Continue blending subsequent rows until you reach the desired fullness or coverage. ### **Step 6: Check Placement and Adjustment** Periodically check the placement of the clips while working through different sections of your head. Ensuring proper alignment prevents visible gaps or uneven distribution. If necessary, adjust individual clips for optimal comfort and invisibility. ### **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Start with fewer clips if you're new to applying extensions; practice will make perfect! * Avoid sleeping with clip-in extensions still attached; remove them nightly to preserve both your natural hair and the extensions themselves. * Clean and maintain your extensions regularly according to manufacturer recommendations to prolong their lifespan. * Experiment with various hairstyles to find what works best for disguising the presence of clip-ins. Braids, updos, curls, and waves can help integrate them seamlessly into your look. For an impressionable boy, their inspiration was incalculable. They gave me my first feeling for the Italian style-but I could never say how much they gave me. At that age, such influences are actually creative. Title: How to Get Ideas About Writing a Song Introduction: Writing a song can be an incredibly rewarding experience, but coming up with ideas for your lyrics and melody might seem like a daunting task. However, by following these steps, you'll learn various techniques that will help spark inspiration and guide you through the process of generating unique and captivating song concepts. This article is designed to provide clear guidance and practical advice, enabling you to develop your own original songs using effective brainstorming methods and creativity-boosting strategies. Step 1: Choose a Theme or Topic * Explanation: Selecting a theme or topic gives direction to your writing, making it easier to generate relevant content and maintain focus throughout the creative process. By choosing a subject matter close to your heart, you increase the chances of crafting authentic and engaging material. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Consider personal experiences, emotions, relationships, dreams, aspirations, or social issues as potential themes. + Opt for specific topics rather than broad ones; this allows for more focused storytelling and richer imagery (e.g., choose 'first love' over just 'love'). + Keep your target audience in mind when selecting a theme, ensuring resonance with listeners. Step 2: Freewrite and Journal Entries * Explanation: Engaging in freewriting exercises encourages spontaneous thoughts and feelings related to your chosen theme without worrying about grammar, structure, or rhyme scheme. These activities foster self-expression while simultaneously unlocking hidden insights and perspectives. * Key Tips & Guidelines: + Set aside at least 15 minutes daily to write freely about your selected topic. Telling Time in Different Time Zone Return to Mathematics Index Borita Khim Stockton Elementary School 4420 N. Beacon Chicago IL 60640 To have the 4th grade students learn to tell time in different time zones. Paper plates, fasteners, crayons, scissors, pencils, pens, markers, 2 different colors of construction paper, digital and standard face clocks, transparency of time zone map and time zone maps for class, funtac, envelopes and card boards. 1. Discuss with the students the importance of knowing the time in different places so that people can communicate with each other without having time 2. Show students a standard clock 3. Have students make their own clocks out of the paper plates. 4. Show students the map of the U.S.A. that shows the various time zones. Show time zone map transparency which explains time zone divisions. Read the time on the map with students and discuss the time in different parts of the U.S.A. Give students the map of the U.S.A. that shows the various time 5. Have students turn their clocks to the current time and ask students if the current time in California is the same as the time in Chicago. Wait for students to respond. Ask questions about various time zones. 6. Group students into six groups. Each group represents a different time zone (Hawaii, Alaska, Pacific, Mountain, Central, and Eastern). 7. Instruct groups to turn their clocks to a specific time in any time zone and have all groups adjust the time to their particular time zones. Then ask them if their time is behind or ahead of the specific time given. Ask questions on various time zones and have groups change zones. 8. Give each group an envelope that contains four state labels. Have the group find the states and the time zones. One of the group members places the labels on the map of the states and tells the time zone and the difference from the time in the Central time zone. 9. Give each group a card board that has two clocks on the top, one indicates the Chicago time and the other one is blank with the word Hawaii on the top. Below the clocks there are questions which ask students to look at the Chicago time and find the Hawaii time and describe what boys and girls would be doing in both states at that time. Solar astronomy is the study of the Sun. The Sun is the closest star to Earth at around 92 million (92,000,000) miles away. It is the easiest to observe in detail. Observing the Sun can help us understand how other stars work and are formed. Changes in the Sun can affect the weather and climate on Earth. A stream of charged particles called the Solar wind is constantly sent off from the Sun. The Solar Wind hitting the Earth's magnetic field causes the northern lights. Studying the Sun helped people understand how nuclear fusion works. The word body is sometimes used in other ways. For example, a lake is a body of water. The laws on a topic are a "body of law". Title: How to Decide Who to Talk to on MSN Introduction: MSN (now known as Windows Live Messenger) is an instant messaging client that allows you to communicate with friends, family, and colleagues worldwide. However, deciding who to talk to can sometimes be challenging, especially when you have many contacts. This tutorial will provide in-depth guidance on determining who to engage in conversation on MSN while considering factors such as your relationship, their availability, shared interests, and communication etiquette. Step 1: Categorize Your Contacts Categorizing your contacts based on various criteria like personal relationships, professional connections, or common hobbies simplifies decision-making. You can create groups within MSN or maintain separate lists outside the application. By categorizing your contacts, you ensure conversations are more meaningful and relevant to both parties. Key tip: Regularly review and update contact categories to reflect any changes in your relationships or mutual interests. Step 2: Determine Availability Check if potential conversational partners are online by looking at their status indicators (e.g., green dot signifying 'available' or red dot indicating 'busy'). Avoid initiating conversations during inconvenient times unless urgent. Respecting others' time demonstrates maturity and consideration. Guideline: If someone appears unavailable, consider sending them an offline message instead. Explain why you wanted to reach out and suggest another suitable time for further discussion. Step 3: Evaluate Shared Interests Consider what activities, topics, or passions you share with specific individuals before starting a chat session. Common ground fosters stronger connections and engaging discussions. For instance, talking about recent movies with a film enthusiast friend makes sense rather than discussing cooking techniques with them. Key Tip: Keep track of significant events or updates related to your contacts' lives (e.g., new job, relocation, birthdays). These details offer excellent icebreaker opportunities and show genuine interest in their wellbeing. Step 4: Consider Communication Purpose Before beginning a conversation, clarify its purpose-whether exchanging pleasantries, seeking advice, sharing news, or organizing social gatherings. Having clear intentions ensures productive interactions without wasting time on irrelevant subjects. Guideline: Refrain from bombarding multiple people simultaneously with similar messages; tailor content according to individual preferences and contexts. Step 5: Observe Appropriate Etiquette Maintaining proper etiquette shows respect for others and enhances overall user experience. Here are some essential guidelines: * Be polite and courteous * Use appropriate language and avoid offensive words * Don't type in ALL CAPS (it implies shouting) * Use punctuation correctly * Limit emoticon usage Key tip: Never disclose sensitive information without prior consent. Privacy should always come first! Step 6: Monitor Response Time Pay attention to response times during chats. While everyone has different pacing habits, excessive delays might indicate disinterest or distraction. Adjust your engagement accordingly or tactfully change the subject if necessary. Guideline: Avoid pressing for immediate responses or getting frustrated by slow replies. Patience goes a long way in maintaining healthy virtual relationships. Conclusion: By following these steps and guidelines, you'll learn how to decide who to talk to on MSN effectively. Remember, successful online communication depends on understanding your audience, respecting boundaries, and nurturing positive relationships through thoughtful interaction. Happy chatting! Look in the bag and see what you find to write on. There are many versions to the story of the Trojan War. Two of the most famous of these stories are Homer's poetry about this war in his books, the Iliad and the Odyssey. Full of exaggerations, distortions, contradictions, and pure fictions, these two books do not give a clear indication whether or not the Trojan war actually occurred. Homer wrote these stories based on the oral tradition of storytelling. Oral tradition has a tendency to not contain precise memories and can morph into different claims based on different cultural influences and intentions. For example, the Iliad is shown to have many similar aspects to characters and wars originating from stories throughout the Hellenic world, like the duel between Lycian Sapedon and Rhodian. These similarities in stories are not originally connected with Troy. This creates fundamental distortion which contains three principles. First, a heroic tradition may be constructed to seem as an event that holds more significance than it truly does. In Herodotus's version of the Trojan War, he says "his narrative is presented as belonging to the realm of 'what is said' rather than 'what is known." He tries to present a credible source of the story by eliminating the gods by making Helen be the daughter of a human, Tyndareus, rather than the daughter of a god. He also makes Alexander (Paris) not judge on the beauty of the three goddesses and become convinced by Aphrodite's promise to marry Helen. Instead, he steals Helen. He does this because humans decide on their own what is and is not credible about ancient civilizations. He knew the popular opinion would be that people do not see supernatural as credible but instead as fiction. Secondly, tradition may be picked up by regions and people who have no relation to the event. For example, different civilizations would use the information as "propagandistic purposes" to make their civilization look heroic or enemy civilizations remain monstrous. The process of passing down stories through word of mouth as the only source leads to inevitable change to the truth in the story through the different cultural influences of those telling the story. For example, if the Spartans were telling their perspective of the Trojan war compared to the Trojans, then the two stories will most likely be very different. Thirdly, the traditions may become distorted in time including the original main idea so that it is not recognizable from the rest of the evidence. This means that there most likely was a Trojan war. However, the war was not the same as what Homer states. The story is known to be part of "spatium" "histoicum" which is the clear belonging of a deep past in which accurate knowledge is difficult to obtain. For example there is also no written evidence to validate the Trojan war and archeologists can not yet provide evidence of who attacked Troy. This makes the storyline much easier to change and is why many researchers believe the Trojan war stories are about war with enemies created as a result of a copper shortage in Greece. At the time, the Trojans were the only ones that would have access to the copper supply in the Back Sea. Overall, these variations in the story of the Trojan war are not reliable sources to aid figuring out whether the Trojan war happened or not; however, they help give an understanding of the cultural background and intentions of the people during this era. They are mostly in Australia and New Guinea, and some are on the smaller islands. **Please note that advance registration is required for this event** Preparing for Kindergarten (Parent Guide) Children are unique individuals and move through developmental stages at their own rates. We must be careful however, not to expect all children to move at the same pace as others. Young children are naturally curious. They learn by experiencing, moving, and using their senses. Parental involvement in a child's learning is so important. The following suggestions are activities that you can use to encourage your child's learning. Read to your child often. Let your child choose a story and you choose one that you enjoy also. Help your child "read" signs in the community, e.g., fast food restaurants, grocery stores, road signs, and labels on food cartons. Talk with your child. Children learn about the world through language. Talk about things you are going to do before you do it and, afterward, talk about what you did. Tell stories about favorite events and activities. Help your child create mental images about these activities by drawing pictures about them. Take photographs of a family outing and together arrange them in order in a scrapbook. Write a short caption about what is happening in the photos. Help your child recognize his or her own name. Write family names on index cards or any paper and play games guessing names. Let your child help you write and read your grocery store list. Show your child what you are reading and what your choices are. Cut letters from the newspaper, magazines, catalogs, or boxes to help your child make words. Find numbers on signs and billboards while traveling. Ask your child to identify the numbers. Place five objects on a table. Remove one while your child is not watching. Ask your child to tell you what is missing and how many are left. Have your child help you set the table for a meal (one-to-one correspondence). Create a clapping and snapping pattern with your child. Ask him or her what comes next. Fill five glasses with different levels of water. Ask your child to arrange the glasses from least amount to the greatest amount. Practice reading numbers and dialing phone numbers of family members and friends. Ask your child to close his or her eyes. Place objects one at a time in your child's hand and ask him/her to describe the object by texture, weight, size, and shape. Help your child place family photos in order from birth to present. Collect a box of shoes and then sort them by men's and women's, by color, or by lace-ups and Velcro. Have your child draw pictures that represent the numbers from 1-10. "We better keep some sand to play with another day!" the boy said. So they put the rest of the sand back in the bucket and waved goodbye. In addition to political control, the United States also exercised significant control over the Cuban economy. At the time, Cuba was a monoculture economy. While they produced coffee, tobacco, and rice, they relied primarily on sugar. Thus, they were known by other countries as the "sugar bowl of the world." The United States bought sugar from the Republic of Cuba at a price higher than the global standard. In exchange, Cuba was to give preference to the United States, and its industries. Cuba depended on the United States and their investments. Cuba was not industrialized and needed the revenue for goods and oil. They also needed the US investment for gas, electricity, communications, railways, and banks. While Cuban workers had better conditions than other countries in the continent, they still faced inequality, lack of infrastructure, high illiteracy rates, and a lack of full-time work (the sugar industry was seasonal). will you have it with two hands which one is your right hand? To learn to read and spell words children must learn phonemes and letter correspondences. Sometimes a phoneme is represented by two letters that go together to make one sound such as /sh/. This is called a diagraph and is very common in the English language. The following lesson will help children practice learning the /sh/ sound when they see s and h together in print. Elkin Letterboxes, letters: s, h, o, p, i, f, d, c, r, b, u, p, s, One Fish, Two Fish, Red Fish, Blue Fish by Dr. Suess, picture page 1. Introduce the lesson by explaining how some letters are placed together to make one sound. To read we must be able to tell when a sound is being made by two letters such as /sh/. 2. How many children have been told by their parents. "SSSSHHH! You are being too loud!" Me too. Now lets be the parents and make the /sh/ sound together by pushing air out our mouth with our teeth together. 3. We will practice with a tongue twister: Shelly showed Shane shells she found on the shore. Say it with me this time. Wonderful! Now say it one more time stretching out the /sh/ sound. SSSHHHelly ssshhhowed SSSHHHane ssshhhells ssshhhe found on the ssshhhore. 4. Letterbox lesson: Pass out the letters and have the students turn the letters to lower case side. Take tape and connect the s and h to make /sh/. Review and ask, "Does anyone remember what sound this makes?" ( /sh/ Excellent!) Now I will demonstrate with the word ship (sh 1st box, i 2nd box, p 3rd box) 5. Have students spell out shop, fish, and dish. Open another box for crash and brush, and as a final challenge open one more for splash. 6. On the board spell out words and have the students read aloud . 7. In groups read One Fish, Two Fish, Red Fish, Blue Fish by Dr. Suess. Have them go back and point out all /sh/ words. 8. For assessment have students circle and write the name of pictures containing /sh/ from a picture sheet. The worksheet will be corrected aloud when everyone is finished. Reference: Reading Genie www.auburn.edu/rdggenie www.auburn.edu/rdggenie/illum/jonesbr.html Murray, B.A. & Lesniak, T (1999). The Letterbox Lesson. The Reading Teacher, 53,644-650. You can tell me what the word means bicycle. Adding a videos link to the start menu on Windows 7 is an easy way to quickly access your video files or folders. By following these steps, you can customize your start menu to better suit your needs. Step 1: Access the Start Menu To begin, click on the Start button located at the bottom left corner of your screen. This will open up the Start Menu, which provides quick access to programs, documents, settings, and more. Step 2: Open the Control Panel Next, locate and click on the "Control Panel" option within the Start Menu. The Control Panel allows users to adjust various system settings, including personalization options like customizing the Start Menu. Step 3: Navigate to Personalization Options Once inside the Control Panel, look for the "Appearance and Personalization" category. Click on this category to expand its contents, then select "Personalization." This will take you to a page where you can modify various visual aspects of your computer's interface. Step 4: Customize the Start Menu In the Personalization window, find the section labeled "Start Menu," and click on the "Customize" button beneath it. A new window with several configuration options will appear. Step 5: Create a New Folder Now that you have accessed the customization settings for the Start Menu, scroll down until you see the "Start Menu Size" heading. Beneath this heading, there are two checkboxes related to creating new folders. Check both boxes next to "Number of recent programs to display" and "Number of items to display in Jump Lists". These settings allow you to create a new folder specifically for your videos links. Key Tip: Make sure you leave enough space between other existing shortcuts so as not to overcrowd the Start Menu. An organized layout makes navigation easier and faster. Step 6: Specify Number of Items After checking the necessary boxes, specify the number of items (programs) you want to be displayed under the newly created folder. For instance, if you plan to add ten video links, set the value to 10. Remember that setting too high a number might make navigating through your Start Menu challenging due to clutter. Guideline: As a rule of thumb, aim for a balance between convenience and organization; don't overwhelm yourself with too many choices at once. Step 7: Save Changes Once you have configured the desired settings, click the "Apply" button followed by "OK" to save changes. Your new folder should now be visible in the Start Menu. Step 8: Name Your Folder Hover over the newly-created folder, and right-click when the icon turns into a four-way arrow. Select "Rename" from the contextual menu, type 'Videos', and press Enter. You may choose another name relevant to your specific collection of video files or folders. Step 9: Pin Video Files or Folders Finally, populate your new 'Videos' folder by pinning applicable files or directories containing video content. To do this, navigate to the location of your video files via File Explorer, right-click on the file(s)/folder(s), and hover over "Pin to Start Menu." Choose either "Pin to Start Menu" (for individual files) or "Pin to Taskbar" (for folders). Afterward, they'll show up under your recently added 'Videos' folder. By completing these nine steps, you have successfully added a dedicated videos link to your Start Menu in Windows 7! Enjoy having quicker access to your favorite video content without needing to browse through multiple layers of directories. oh, they must be furious with that cat, huh? Title: How to Be an Amazing Mom Introduction: Becoming an amazing mom is a journey filled with love, patience, and dedication. It requires constant learning, adapting, and growing alongside your children. This guide will provide you with steps and tips to help you build a strong, positive relationship with your kids while fostering their development and well-being. Remember that there's no one-size-fits-all approach to parenting, so feel free to modify these suggestions according to your unique family situation. Step 1: Cultivate a Strong Emotional Bond * Importance: A strong emotional bond between mother and child forms the foundation for trust, security, and healthy attachment. * Tips: + Spend quality time together: Engage in activities that both you and your child enjoy, creating shared memories and strengthening your connection. + Show empathy and validate feelings: Recognize and respond appropriately to your child's emotions, helping them develop emotional intelligence and resilience. + Practice active listening: Give your full attention when your child speaks, asking open-ended questions and summarizing their thoughts to show understanding. Step 2: Prioritize Your Child's Physical Health and Safety * Importance: Ensuring your child's physical health and safety provides a stable environment for growth and exploration. * Guidelines: + Establish routines: Create consistent schedules for sleeping, eating, and other daily activities to promote stability and predictability. + Provide proper nutrition: Offer balanced meals and snacks with plenty of fruits, vegetables, whole grains, lean proteins, and dairy products. Limit processed foods and sugary drinks. + Encourage physical activity: Promote regular exercise through play, sports, or outdoor activities suitable for your child's age and interests. + Teach safety rules: Educate your child about potential hazards at home, school, and public places, emphasizing traffic safety, stranger danger, and online etiquette. [19 sec S grunts once, then turns on his recorder and music starts] Title: How to Construct a Triangle When Two Sides and the Included Angle Are Given (SAS Criterion) Introduction: In geometry, constructing a triangle with given elements is an essential skill. This tutorial will focus on constructing a triangle when two sides and the included angle are provided, also known as the SAS criterion. By following these steps, you'll be able to create accurate triangles using only compass and straightedge tools. Let's begin! What You Need: - A pair of compasses - A ruler or straightedge - Paper Step 1: Draw the First Side Using your ruler, draw a line segment representing one of the given sides. Label its endpoints as points A and B. The length should match the measurement of the side specified in the problem. Key Tip: Make sure both ends of the line segment are labeled properly, so you can keep track of them throughout the construction process. Step 2: Set Compass to the Length of the Second Side Adjust the opening width of your compass to equal the length of the second side. It doesn't matter which endpoint you choose from Step 1; let's say we select point A. Mark this distance along the edge of the compass and make sure that the pencil tip is at the correct setting. Guideline: Always double-check the accuracy of your compass settings before marking any new points to avoid errors during construction. Step 3: Swing the Arc Through Point B Place the needlepoint of your compass firmly on point B while keeping the pencil tip at the set distance. Keeping the needlepoint steady, swing the compass around to create an arc intersecting the first line segment. Label the intersection point as point C. Congratulations! You now have two vertices (A and C) connected by the required side length. Key Information: At this stage, you may notice that there could potentially be more than one possible location for point C if the arc intersected the original line segment twice. However, since you know the angle between the two sides, you can narrow down the options in later steps. Step 4: Setting Up the Included Angle Without changing the current compass setting, place the needlepoint back on point C. Now rotate the compass slowly until the pencil tip touches point A again. Be careful to maintain the same distance setting on your compass throughout this motion. Step 5: Creating the Angle Once the compass has been adjusted correctly, carefully lift the needlepoint off the paper without altering the position of the pencil tip. Then, place the needlepoint gently on point A while ensuring that the pencil tip remains touching point C. Gently press down on the needlepoint to secure its placement, creating a small circular indentation on the paper. Finally, remove the compass and connect points A and C with a straight line using your ruler. Congratulations! You have successfully constructed the included angle ( BAC). Step 6: Finalizing the Construction Since you already have all three points and two sides defined, simply join the remaining unconnected points with lines drawn using your ruler. Specifically, draw a line connecting points B and C. With that, you have completed the construction of the triangle according to the given criteria (two sides and their included angle). Conclusion: By mastering the art of constructing triangles based on various criteria, such as the SAS criterion outlined herein, you become proficient in geometrical constructions-a fundamental skillset applicable across numerous mathematical disciplines. Practice makes perfect; soon enough, you'll find yourself effortlessly crafting precise shapes like a pro! Hello young mathematicians! Today we have a fun challenge involving numbers and trigonometry - specifically, averaging sine values! Don't worry if these words sound complicated; we will make it easy and enjoyable using things you see every day. Imagine you are observing a clock hand moving over 100 equal intervals during its full rotation. Each interval represents an angle between 0 and 360 , measured in degrees. Now let's take our friend, the sine function, which gives us the y-coordinate of a point on the unit circle for any given angle. We want to calculate the **average value** of `sin(100x)` within a 10% margin of error. Sounds tricky? Let's break it down together! #### Breaking Down The Problem We all love playing with toys and sorting them into groups, so imagine each degree measurement as a different toy in your collection. You need to find out the average "happiness factor," or Y coordinate, of all those toys when placed along the edge of a big circular board - our unit circle! First, compute the sine value for each toy, i.e., angle from 1 to 100 . Remember, sine values range between -1 and +1, making up the y-coordinates on our imaginary circle. Once you have calculated these values, simply add them up and divide by the total number of angles (which is 100). That final result is your average happiness factor, but remember, we want it to be accurate within a 10% margin. #### Why Should We Care? This exercise helps build essential skills like calculating means, understanding periodic functions, and visualizing concepts in space. Plus, once you get comfortable solving similar challenges, higher-level math topics won't seem intimidating anymore! Just think, you could become a pro at analyzing wave patterns, designing roller coasters, or creating animations! So grab your pencil and paper, and start having some fun crunching those numbers! Remember, there's no better way to learn than diving headfirst into exciting problems. Good luck, happy math-ing, and may the averages always be in your favor!
