Tutorial: How to Cook Sharks in RuneScape RuneScape is a popular online multiplayer game that offers various activities, including cooking. One of the most sought-after dishes in the game is shark, which requires level 90 cooking. This tutorial will guide you through the process of cooking sharks in RuneScape with clear and practical steps. Step 1: Gathering Raw Sharks To cook sharks, first, you need to obtain raw sharks. You can catch them using a fishing level of at least 76 and a harpoon or higher-level weapon. Alternatively, you can buy raw sharks from other players or purchase them from Grand Exchange. Having a large number of raw sharks ensures efficient training and profitability. Key Tip: It's more cost-effective to fish your own sharks if you have high enough Fishing level. However, buying raw sharks may be faster and more convenient depending on market prices. Step 2: Prepare Your Character Before starting to cook sharks, ensure your character has the required stats. The minimum cooking level needed is 90; otherwise, attempting to cook sharks will result in failure and loss of both the shark and some firemaking experience points. If you don't have a high enough cooking level, consider raising it by practicing on lower-leveled food like trout or salmon until you reach level 90. Additionally, make sure your inventory isn't full so there's room for cooked sharks. Also, equip any gear or items that boost your cooking skill, such as cooking gauntlets (from completing the Family Crest quest), chef's hat, or spicy stew. These bonuses increase your chances of successfully cooking sharks. Guideline: Carry water sources like jugs of water or buckets of water to extinguish fires when necessary. Step 3: Locate a Suitable Range A range is an essential piece of equipment used for cooking. There are several ranges scattered across Gielinor where you can cook sharks. Some notable locations include Lumbridge Castle kitchen, Varrock West Bank, Falador Kitchen, and Catherby bank. Choose one closest to your current location or near a teleport option for quick access. Step 4: Start Cooking! Interact with the range and select 'Cook'. Then, add up to 28 raw sharks into the inventory slots above the action buttons. Wait for the animation to finish before removing the cooked sharks from the range. Repeat this process until all raw sharks are cooked or your inventory becomes full. Remember: Monitor the progress bar while cooking to avoid burning any sharks. Burning occurs randomly but increases if your cooking level is too low compared to the item being cooked. Key Tips: * Use multiple inventories worth of raw sharks instead of cooking only small amounts at once to maximize efficiency. * Keep track of your success rate and adjust accordingly. Lower your stack size if you notice many burnt sharks. * Consider banking after every few trips to sell excess cooked sharks or restock raw sharks. By following these steps, you should now be able to efficiently cook sharks in RuneScape. Enjoy leveling up your cooking skills and reaping the rewards of this delicious dish! A cin camera or movie camera takes a rapid sequence of photographs on image sensor or strips of photographic film. Unlike a still camera the cin camera takes a series of images, each one is called a "frame". The frames are later played back in a cin projector at a specific speed, called the "frame rate" (number of frames per second). While viewing, human visual system merges the separate pictures to create the illusion of motion. The first cin camera was built around 1888. By 1890 several types were being made. The standard film size for cin cameras was quickly established as 35mm film. This remained in use until transition to digital cinematography. So um he was not at all interested in the stuffed animals. [a string on nonsense and laughing follows] Phase One: Planting the Seed When a seed starts to grow, we say it germinates. The cotyledons store food for the baby plant inside the seed. When the seed starts to germinate, the first thing to grow is the main root. The seed's growing conditions usually need to be damp, warm, and dark, like springtime soil. A dry seed will stay dormant until it soaks in some water, then it will start to germinate. There are two different types of seeds: dicotyledons and monocotyledons. Dicotyledons are seeds with two parts and monocotyldons have only one part. You can call theses seed types dicots and monocots for short. An example of a dicot would be a bean seed. A bean seed that has been soaked in water has a soft outer covering. This outer covering is the seed coat. Inside of the seed would be a tiny plant called the embryo. The two large parts of the seed are called the cotyledons. The cotyledons are stored food that the young plant will use while it is growing. Monocots are seeds that have only one cotyledon, such as the corn seed. Monocots have a thicker seed coat that does not slip off easily like the bean seed. The corn seed does not split open like the bean seed, it stays in one piece. One cotyldeon surrounds the embryo and is called the endosperm. Phase One Vocabulary Definitions: Seed Coat - Protects the inside of the seed. Embryo - the beginning of the new plant. Endosperm - Stored food in a monocotyledon. Stored Food - a plant uses stored food until it grows leaves that can make food. Monocot - a type of seed that has only one cotyledon. Dicot - a type of seed that has two cotyledons. Cotyledon - Leaf-like structure that absorbs and digest food stored in the seed. Germination - the process of growing a new plant from a seed. Title: How to Count Cells on Google Sheets on Android Google Sheets is a powerful tool that allows you to create, edit, and collaborate on spreadsheets from your Android device. One common task when working with spreadsheets is counting cells that meet certain criteria. This tutorial will guide you through the process of counting cells on Google Sheets using an Android device. Step 1: Open the Google Sheets App Locate and open the Google Sheets app on your Android device. If you don't have the app installed yet, download it from the Google Play Store. Step 2: Access Your Spreadsheet Tap on 'Open files' if the file is already saved in your Google Drive or tap on the '+' icon at the bottom right corner to create a new one. Navigate through your folders and select the desired spreadsheet. Key Tip: Make sure your spreadsheet has data filled in the cells as the count function won't work on empty sheets. Step 3: Navigate to the Data Range Once you've opened your spreadsheet, navigate to the area where you want to perform the cell count operation. Use your finger to scroll up, down, left, or right until you reach the desired location. Guideline: It's important to know exactly which range of cells you need to include in your count because including unnecessary rows or columns could lead to incorrect results. Step 4: Tap on an Empty Cell Select any blank cell near the data range where you wish to display the count result. By doing this, you prepare a space for inputting the COUNTA or COUNT functions. Step 5: Enter the COUNTA Function (for non-empty cells) Tap on the selected cell to activate editing mode. Type '=COUNTA(' without hitting enter yet. The COUNTA function counts all non-empty cells within a specified range. Alternatively, if you only want to count numeric values, use the COUNT function instead ('=COUNT('.). Step 6: Specify the Data Range Now, identify the top-left and bottom-right corners of the data range by tapping on their respective column letters and row numbers. For example, if you want to count cells A1 through D10, type 'A1:D10'. Remember to close the parentheses after specifying the range. So, your formula should look like this: =COUNTA(A1:D10) or =COUNT(A1:D10), depending on what kind of count you desire. Step 7: Apply the Function Hit 'Enter' on your keyboard to apply the function. After entering the formula correctly, the previously blank cell will now show the total number of cells containing data within the defined range. Key Tips: * Be careful about spaces before or after equal signs since these can cause errors. * Double-check your formulas carefully before pressing enter. In conclusion, following these steps enables you to effectively count cells on Google Sheets using your Android device. Whether you are tracking inventory, monitoring progress, or analyzing data trends, knowing how to count cells comes in handy in many situations. Happy computing! What do you put in the cup? Some waves can move through matter while others cannot. For instance, some waves can move through empty space, light waves for example. Sound waves, on the other hand, cannot move through empty space. Inherently, all waves carry energy from one place to another when they move. Samurai were allowed to fight anyone who did not show them proper respect. Every sword had to be tested. To do this, the owner of the sword could execute a criminal. This allowed the warrior to test his sword's sharpness. Samurai led their lives according to the ethical code of "bushido" ("the way of the warrior"). WHALES IN THE SEA Try one or both of these art ideas with your group. || Invite the children to fingerpaint blue waves all over pieces of fingerpaint paper. When the paint has dried, give the children gray or black construction paper cutouts of a whale to glue onto their papers. || Let older children trace around whale cookie cutters on gray construction paper and cut out the shapes to glue onto their ocean waves. Encourage them to make several cutouts to create a pod. Let each of your children paint blue waves on the front side of a thin paper plate. Cut a slit across the middle of each plate, leaving a 2-inch margin at each end of the slit. Have each child glue a 3 -inch whale shape, cut from heavy paper, onto one end of a craft stick. Demonstrate how to work the puppet by inserting the other end of the craft stick down through the slit on the painted side of the plate and moving the stick up and down on the back side of the plate to make the whale dive in and out of the waves. Let the children use their puppets to accompany whale songs and stories. Select five large index cards. Cut each card into two puzzle pieces, making sure that each puzzle fits together differently. On the left-hand piece of each puzzle, write a numeral from 1 to 5, and on the righ-hand piece, rubber-stamp a matching number of whale prints. Mix up all the puzzle pieces, place them in a pile, and let your children put the puzzles back together. Cut a mother whale shape and a baby whale, or calf, shape out of several different colors of felt. Place the mother shapes on a flannelboard and give the calf shapes to your group. Ask the children to help the calves find their mothers by matching their colors. Then have them place the calves alongside their mothers on the flannelboard. Cut several sizes of whales ranging from small to large out of heavy gray or black paper. Place the shapes in a pile. Then let your children take turns lining up the whales from smallest to largest or from largest to smallest. If you wish, have them line up the whales on a blue paper "ocean." || Use picture books to show and discuss the varieties of whales and their characteristics. Explain that whales are mammals and point out how they differ from fish. || The largest whale of all, the blue whale, measures 100 feet in length. Take your children outdoors and measure off 100 feet on a sidewalk. || Put on some music and let the children pretend to be whales diving down into the water and swimming back up again to spout. || Play a recording of whale sounds and ask the children to make up stories about what the whales are saying. Write down their ideas and use them to make a group whale book. HAVE YOU SEEN THE BIG GRAY WHALE? The big gray whale, the big gray whale. Yes, I've seen the big gray whale. He waved his tail at me! More verses: "Have you seen the orca whale; Have you seen the big blue whale," and so forth. || Use whale cookie cutters to make cookies. Let your children decorate the cookies with frosting and sprinkles. || Invite the children to make placemats for snacktime. Have them trace around whale cookie cutters on pieces of construction paper and then color the whale shapes with crayons. The following series of pronunciation exercises combines words beginning with the same consonant sound followed by similar vowel sounds. Voiced and voiceless consonants are paired (b - voiced / p - voiceless, d - voiced / t - voiceless, etc.) to help students compare and contrast similar consonant formation. - Repeat each line slowly, listen for the minor differences between the vowel and consonant sounds. - Repeat each line three times. Each time repeat more quickly trying to keep the sounds distinct. - Find a partner and listen to each other repeat the lines. - Try to invent sentences using each sound at least once. For example: The man at the bar bought drinks for all. - Don't worry too much about the sentence making much sense! |'long ah' as in 'heart'||'short ah' as in 'got'| 'eh' - as in 'let', 'ih' - as in 'hit', 'ee' - as in 'see', and 'ae'- as in 'cat' 'long ah' - as in 'car', 'short ah' - as in 'got' 'long uh' - as in 'put', 'short uh' - as in 'up', 'oo' - as in 'through' Now he wasn't hungry anymore and he wasn't a little caterpillar anymore. Are you going to eat the soap? The next most important part of a computer, after memory, is the processor. A processor changes the values in memory as instructed by instructions stored in memory. An instruction is a group of bits (loop values) in memory that tell the processor to do something. A group of instructions that instruct the processor to do some task is called a program. The simple kind of processor described in this book has only one type of instruction, but that instruction is sufficient to do anything, as will be seen. The instruction is 'copy' (and 'go to'). Each instruction simply copies some bits of data from somewhere in one latch in memory to somewhere else in another latch in memory. The instruction indicates:1. which latch to copy data (data is bit (loop) values) from 2. which latch to copy data to 3. which bits to change in the copied-to latch 4. how much to rotate the 'from' data before copying some of its bits to the 'to' data latch 5. which latches to get the next instruction from. In the computer considered here, the number of address bits is the same as the number of data bits. The explanation of the following example will not be clear at first, but just read through it. Then reread it. It will be clear later. Consider a four-address-bit, four-data-bit memory with the values (in loops) below: latch bit address values 0000 0100 address of instruction 0001 0001 value of a 0010 0001 not 0 = 1 0011 0000 not 1 = 0 instruction_1 0100 0001 from address 0101 1000 to address 0110 0001 'to' bits to change 0111 1000 instr.addr.and rot.amount instruction_2 1000 0010 from address 1001 0001 to address 1010 0001 'to' bits to change 1011 1100 instr.addr.and rot.amount instruction_3 1100 0000 from address 1101 0000 to address 1110 0000 'to' bits to change 1111 1100 instr.addr.and rot.amount Only the 1's and 0's are part of the program. The rest is just comments for a person. The latch addresses are just where the program is stored. The bit values are the program. 'Instr.addr.and rot.amount' is short for 'next instruction's address and rotate amount.' Latch 0000 holds the value 0100 so that the first instruction is in latches 0100, 0101, 0110, and 0111, and is labeled 'instruction_1' in the program. Latch 0000 is special and always holds the address of the next instruction to be executed. The first word of instruction_1 is in latch 0100 and is 0001. That means that data (bit values) is copied from latch 0001 in memory. The second word of instuction_1 is in latch 0101 and is 1000 and indicates that the data will be copied to latch 1000. The third word of instruction_1 is in latch 0110 and is 0001 and indicates that only the rightmost bit, and not the three leftmost bits, of latch 1000 will be changed because only the rightmost bit of 0001 is 1. The rightmost two bits of latch 0111 are 00 and indicate that the data in latch 0001 will not be rotated at all when data is copied to latch 1000. The leftmost two bits of latch 0111 are 10 and indicate that the next instruction will be in latches 1000, 1001, 1010, and 1011. That is, instruction_2 will be executed next. After instruction_1 is executed, the memory has the following bit values.latch bit address values 0000 1000 <-----| address of instruction 0001 0001 ---| | value of a 0010 0001 | | not 0 = 1 0011 0000 | | not 1 = 0 instruction_1 0100 0001 | | from address 0101 1000 | | to address 0110 0001 | | 'to' bits to change 0111 1000 ---+--| instr.addr.and rot.amount instruction_2 1000 0011 <--| from address 1001 0001 to address 1010 0001 'to' bits to change 1011 1100 instr.addr.and rot.amount instruction_3 1100 0000 from address 1101 0000 to address 1110 0000 'to' bits to change 1111 1100 instr.addr.and rot.amount The underlined loop values (bits) were copied to when instruction_1 was executed. The italics show from where data was copied. The arrows show how data was copied. The rightmost bit of latch 0001 has been copied to the rightmost bit of latch 1000. Also, all bits of latch 0111 (that is, 1000) have been copied to latch 0000 indicating that the next instruction will be in latches 1000, 1001, 1010, and 1011 (instruction_2). That is, after the first instruction, instruction_1, is executed, latch 0000 has value 1000. The leftmost two bits of latch 0000 are 10, so the instruction executed next is instruction_2, in latches 1000, 1001, 1010, and 1011. 1. Latch 1000 holds 0011, so data is copied from latch 0011. 2. Latch 1001 holds 0001, so data is copied to latch 0001. 3. Latch 1010 holds 0001, so only the rightmost bit of the 'to latch,' latch 0001, is copied to. 4. Latch 1011 holds 1100. The rightmost two bits of 1100 are 00 so the data copied from is not rotated at all. The leftmost two bits of 1100 are 11, so the next instruction to be executed will be in latches 1100, 1101, 1110, and 1111 (instruction_3). Page 11 . . . Page 1 . . . When they parted, she kissed her Tony many times. I'm sorry you wanted to eat the block, instead of using that hand to wave. Earth's Magnetic Poles Earth has a magnetic field. If you imagine a gigantic bar magnet inside of Earth, you'll have a pretty good idea what Earth's magnetic field is shaped like. Of course, Earth DOESN'T have a giant bar magnet inside it; instead, our planet's magnetic field is made by swirling motions of molten iron in Earth's outer core. Earth has two geographic poles: the North Pole and the South Pole. They are the places on Earth's surface that Earth's imaginary spin axis passes through. Our planet also has two magnetic poles: the North Magnetic Pole and the South Magnetic Pole. The magnetic poles are near, but not quite in the same places as, the geographic poles. The needle in a compass points towards a magnetic pole. When you are far away from a pole a compass is very helpful if you want to find your way around. The compass needle points pretty much due North (or South if you live in the Southern Hemisphere!). However, if you are near either pole, a compass becomes useless. It points towards the magnetic pole, not the true geographic pole. Those two poles could be quite far apart, and in different directions. Think how hard it must have been for early explorers to find their way around in the Arctic and Antarctic without being able to use a compass! Earth's magnetic field is tilted a little bit. If we pretend that Earth's magnetic field is made by a giant bar magnet, that bar magnet would make an angle with Earth's spin axis. That angle is about 11 . That's why the magnetic poles and the geographic poles are not in the same place. If you were standing at one of the magnetic poles, the magnetic field lines would be straight up and down. If you were holding a compass and turned it sideways, its needle would aim straight up and down! Earth's magnetic poles are actually pretty far from its geographic poles. In 2005, the North Magnetic Pole (NMP) was about 810 km (503 miles) from the Geographic North Pole. The NMP was in the Arctic Ocean north of Canada. The South Magnetic Pole (SMP) was about 2,826 km (1,756 miles) from the Geographic South Pole. The SMP was off the coast of Antarctica in the direction of Australia. Did you notice how we said where the magnetic poles were in 2005? Guess what; the magnetic poles actually move around! Remember, swirling motions of molten metal in Earth's outer core make our planet's magnetic field. Those swirling motions are changing all the time. That means the magnetic field is changing, so the magnetic poles move! In the first part of the 20th century, the poles usually moved about 9 km (5.6 miles) per year. Then, around 1970, they started moving faster. In recent years they have been moving about 41 km (25 miles) per year! Sometimes Earth's magnetic field even flips over! The North and South Magnetic Poles trade places. This doesn't happen very often; usually at least a few hundred thousand years pass between these flips. Speaking of flipping, did you know that Earth's North Magnetic Pole is actually a south pole? Huh, what? When compasses were first invented, people noticed that one end of the compass pointed towards the North. They called the end of the compass needle that pointed North the "north end" of the needle (makes sense!). Later, people learned more about magnets. They learned that like ends (a north and a north OR a south and a south) push away from each other. They learned that opposite ends (such as a north and a south) pull toward each other. The needle of a compass is a tiny bar magnet. The north end of the needle is pulled toward Earth's North Magnetic Pole. So the North Magnetic Pole is actually the south pole of Earth's magnetic field. Doesn't that just make your brain ache! The aurora (Northern and Southern Lights) mostly happen near the magnetic poles. That's because the charged particles (mostly electrons and protons) that cause the aurora follow along magnetic field lines towards the magnetic poles. When the particles run into air in Earth's atmosphere, the air glows in pretty colors - making the beautiful aurora! It would be whatever one I grabbed. She picked the oranges and shared them with her friends. They all enjoyed the sweet oranges and thanked the little girl for teaching them how to grow them. From that day on, the little girl became known as the orange girl of the village. Try these ideas to help your child with comprehension: Encourage your child to form pictures in his head of what he is reading. Title: How to Deliver a Roundhouse Kick in Taekwondo Introduction: The roundhouse kick is one of the most fundamental kicks in Taekwondo. It's versatile, powerful, and can be used both offensively and defensively. This article will guide you through delivering a perfect roundhouse kick with proper form and technique. Remember, mastering any martial art move requires consistent practice and patience. Step 1: Stance and Chamber Start by standing in a fighting stance (also known as ready position). Your stronger leg should be at the back, slightly bent. The front foot points forward while the rear foot faces sideways. This stance provides balance and mobility during execution. Chambering involves bringing your knee up towards your chest before extending your leg outward. Start by shifting your weight onto your front leg. Then raise your rear knee so that your thigh is parallel to the floor. Keep your shin vertical; do not lean forward or backward. Proper chambering ensures speed, control, and precision when kicking. Key Tip: Maintain good posture throughout this process. A straight spine allows better transfer of energy from your body into the kick. Step 2: Pivot and Rotate As you lift your knee, pivot on the ball of your supporting foot (front foot), turning it perpendicular to the target. At the same time, rotate your upper body in the direction of the kick, twisting at the waist. Both these actions help generate more power and reach. Guidepoint: Imagine there's an invisible line connecting your belly button to your target. As you pivot and rotate, try to keep your eyes focused on this point. Step 3: Extension and Contact Extend your leg fully once it reaches its highest point during rotation. Visualize snapping a towel. Snap your hips along with the extension to maximize force. Make contact using the instep (top part) of your foot - imagine kicking with the bone between your big toe and second toe. Common Mistake: Avoid swinging your leg like a baseball bat. Instead, focus on quick, controlled movements. Step 4: Recovery and Balance After making contact, quickly retract your leg back to the starting position. Don't just drop your leg down after kicking; instead, actively pull it back under you. Return to your original stance promptly for immediate readiness to counterattack if necessary. Remember: Good balance is crucial in executing consecutive techniques effectively. Practice Tips: 1. Begin practicing slowly to get comfortable with the movement pattern without risking injury. Gradually increase speed as your proficiency improves. 2. Practice against a heavy bag or pad held by a partner to understand the impact and resistance associated with real-life application. 3. Regular stretching routines enhance flexibility needed for high kicks. Safety Guidelines: 1. Always warm up properly before training. 2. Never attempt full-power kicks until you have sufficient control over the technique. 3. Train under professional supervision whenever possible. By following these steps consistently and adhering to safety measures, you'll soon develop a strong, accurate roundhouse kick essential for effective self-defense and competition success in Taekwondo! Why don't you put the shirt on the boy? But see now look it see this pencil eraser? Under the other chair by the window. Combined Rates questions can be daunting for anyone taking the GMAT. Let's take a look at this sample question and detailed solution. Challenge yourself by trying out the problem first before reviewing the solution. Car X began traveling at an average speed of 35 miles per hour. After 72 minutes, car Y began traveling at an average speed of 49 miles per hour. When both cars had traveled the same distance, both cars stopped. How many miles did car X travel from the time car Y began traveling until both cars stopped? Step 1 is to determine how far car X traveled during the 72 minutes before car Y has started. To do this, we first convert 72 minutes to 6/5 of an hour (that's 72/60 simplified), in order to make sure our units match, as our speed is given in miles per hour. Since rate * time = distance, we know that car X traveled 35mph * 6/5 hours = 42 miles. Step 2 we need to determine a relative rate. We do this in order to figure out how many miles car Y gains on car X each hour. To calculate this, subtract the rate of car X from the rate of car Y. 49mph - 35mph = 14mph, which is our relative rate in miles per hour, or the rate that Y gains on X per hour. Step 3 take the distance car Y must gain on car X (that's 42 miles, from step 1) and divide it by the number of miles car Y gains on X in an hour (that's 14 mph from step 2) in order to find the number of hours it will take for both cars to travel the same distance: 42mi/14mph = 3 hours. Finally, we are asked to find how far car X traveled after car Y starts. We use the time we calculated from step 3, which is 3 hours, and multiply it by car X's rate (35mph). This gives us 3hrs * 35mph = 105 miles, which is choice (A) and the correct answer to this problem. Title: How to Delete Your Amazon Search History Amazon is known for keeping track of its users' search history to provide personalized recommendations and improve their shopping experience. However, some people may prefer to delete their search history for privacy reasons or simply to declutter their account. This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting your Amazon search history with clear instructions and helpful tips. **Step 1:** Log into your Amazon Account ------------------------------------- Visit <https://www.amazon.com/> and click on 'Hello, Sign In' at the top right corner of the page. Enter your email address and password associated with your Amazon account to log in. It is essential that you are logged into the correct account where you want to delete the search history. **Step 2:** Access Browsing History Settings ------------------------------------------ Once logged in, hover over the 'Account & Lists' option located near the upper-right side of the webpage (underneath the search bar). A dropdown menu will appear; click on 'Your Browsing History.' Alternatively, visit this link directly - <https://www.amazon.com/hz/yourbrowsinghistory>. **Key Tip**: If you have multiple devices linked to your Amazon account, keep in mind that clearing your browsing history on one device won't affect other devices. You need to perform these steps separately on every device if required. **Step 3:** Manage Your Browsing History -------------------------------------- You will now be taken to the 'Manage Your Browsing History' page displaying all the items you've searched for recently. Each item includes details such as product name, date searched, and an option to remove specific searches from view. ### Substep 3.1: Remove Individual Searches To delete individual searches, locate the item within the list, then click on the 'Remove from View' button next to it. The selected entry should disappear instantly. This method can be time-consuming but offers more control since you get to choose which exact terms remain in your search history. ### Substep 3.2: Clear All Recent Searches At Once If you wish to erase everything quickly, look towards the top left area of the screen, just below the breadcrumb navigation ('Browsing History > Manage Your Browser History'). Click on 'Remove All Items'. Confirm by clicking 'Yes, Delete' when prompted. Please note that doing so permanently removes all recent searches without any chance of recovery. Make sure this is what you intend before proceeding. **Guideline**: Be aware that removing your entire search history might negatively impact the accuracy of recommended products tailored specifically to your preferences. **Step 4:** Turn Off Browsing History Feature (Optional) ---------------------------------------------------- After deleting your search history, you might consider disabling the feature altogether to prevent future accumulation. To do so, find the setting labeled 'Turn off Browsing History' at the top-right corner of the 'Manage Your Browsing History' page. Toggle the switch to OFF position. Disabling this function means that Amazon will no longer store information about your searches. As a result, fewer targeted ads and customized suggestions could appear based on your activities. In conclusion, following these steps allows you to effectively manage and delete your Amazon search history while understanding potential implications. Regularly reviewing and cleaning up your digital footprint promotes online privacy and organization. [picked up a black plastic horse and starts playing with him] Title: How to Perform a Rock to Fakie/Board Stall on a Vert Skateboard Ramp Introduction: Skateboarding is an exciting and challenging sport that requires practice, balance, and determination. One of the fundamental tricks you can learn on a vert skateboard ramp is the rock to fakie/board stall. This trick consists of two parts - first, pumping up the transition (the rock), followed by turning your board around 180 degrees while airborne so that you land back on the ramp backward or "fakie" (the fakie). The board stall involves balancing on top of the coping (the lip) with all four wheels of your skateboard. This wikiHow will guide you through every step needed to perform this impressive trick safely and confidently. Here's a little piece of tree for you. The political demonstrations in autumn-winter 2004 after the Presidential elections gathered millions of people all over the country. On November 26, 2004, Victor Yuschenko lost the Ukrainian presidential election (Viktor Yanukovych was declared winner). However, Yuschenko and his followers argued that the election had been corrupted. They argued that the election results had been falsified by the Ukrainian government, in support of the opposing candidate Victor Yanukovych. They organized political demonstrations in autumn-winter 2004 that gathered millions of people all over the country. They called the demonstrations "The Orange Revolution" (). Former Prime Minister Yulia Tymoshenko was an important ally of Victor Yuschenko during the demonstrations. The Constitutional Court of Ukraine ordered a second round of elections, which Yuschenko won. Title: How to Build Upper Body Strength Introduction: Upper body strength is essential for daily activities such as lifting objects, pushing or pulling doors, and maintaining good posture. Building upper body strength also improves your performance in sports and reduces the risk of injury. This comprehensive guide will walk you through various exercises targeting different muscle groups in your upper body, including chest, back, shoulders, arms, and core muscles that support these areas. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new exercise program. Section 1: Preparation Step 1: Set Clear Goals Define what building upper body strength means to you - increased endurance, bigger muscles, better posture, etc. Setting specific goals will help keep you motivated and focused throughout this process. Step 2: Assess Your Current Fitness Level Perform basic push-ups, pull-ups, dips, and planks to assess your current upper body strength. Knowing where you stand will allow you to track progress effectively and adjust workouts accordingly. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Perform movements slowly and under control during assessment. * Aim for proper form over completing more repetitions. Section 2: Essential Exercises Exercise 1: Push-Ups (Chest) Step 1: Start in high plank position with hands shoulder-width apart and feet hip-distance apart. Step 2: Lower yourself until elbows reach 90 degrees while keeping abs engaged and spine neutral. Step 3: Press back up without locking out elbars at the top. Repeat for desired reps. Exercise 2: Pull-Ups (Back) Step 1: Grab a pull-up bar with an overhand grip wider than shoulder width. Step 2: Hang from the bar with straight arms and engage your core and lats. Step 3: Pull yourself upward till chin clears the bar then lower yourself back down. Repeat for desired reps. Exercise 3: Overhead Press (Shoulders) Step 1: Hold a pair of dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Step 2: Push weights directly above your head until arms are fully extended. Step 3: Slowly return to starting position. Repeat for desired reps. Exercise 4: Bicep Curls (Biceps) Step 1: Stand holding a pair of dumbbells with palms facing forward. Step 2: Keeping elbows close to torso, curl weights towards shoulders. Step 3: Lower weights back down after reaching peak contraction. Repeat for desired reps. English Language - English Grammer: Personal Pronouns (I, you, we, they, he, she, it, me, my, your, our, us, their, them, his, him, her, myself, yourself, ourselves, themselves, himself, herself, itself) EXERCISES for CHAPTER 18. PERSONAL PRONOUNS 1. In the sentences below, fill in the blanks with the personal pronouns which agree with the underlined antecedents. For example: The man walked slowly, because __ was carrying a heavy parcel. The man walked slowly, because he was carrying a heavy parcel. Although ___ knew it was dangerous, the girl wanted to ride the horse. Although she knew it was dangerous, the girl wanted to ride the horse. The tree is very tall, but __ does not give much shade. The tree is very tall, but it does not give much shade. She and I are not coming, because __ are too busy. She and I are not coming, because we are too busy. Doughnuts taste best when ____ are fresh. Doughnuts taste best when they are fresh. 1. The children are happy because _______ have a holiday today. 2. The car is in good condition, but _______ needs a new muffler. 2. This is particularly useful during low-light conditions or at night time. Follow these steps to enable Night Light on your Android Oreo device: **Step 1:** Access Settings App Navigate to your home screen or app drawer and tap on the 'Settings' icon. The settings app allows you to customize various aspects of your device such as sound preferences, display settings, security options etc. **Step 2:** Scroll Down & Tap Display Once inside the settings menu, scroll down until you find 'Display'. It should be located under 'Device' category. Tapping on this option will open up more choices related to your phone's display features like brightness level, adaptive brightness, daydream, font size, wallpaper etc. **Step 3:** Select Night Light Under the 'Device section', tap on 'Night Light'. If you don't see this option immediately after tapping 'Display', keep scrolling down because some devices might place it towards the bottom of the list. Activating Night Light here will help protect your eyes from harmful blue light emitted by the screen. **Step 4:** Turn On Schedule Option After entering into Night Light settings, look for 'Schedule'. A toggle switch next to it indicates whether it's currently active or not. Slide the toggle right to turn it blue which means scheduled mode is now activated. **Step 5:** Set Custom Time Range (Optional) If you want the Night Light function to operate only within specific hours, tap on 'Custom schedule'. Two fields appear - From and To. Fill these boxes with preferred start and end times respectively. For example, if you wish to activate Night Light from sunset to sunrise, there are apps available in Google Play Store that can provide automatic sunrise and sunset timings based on location services. **Step 6:** Adjust Intensity (Optional) For further personalization, select 'Intensity'. Here, you can adjust the amount of warm tone overlay on the screen according to preference. Lower intensity means less reduction of blue light while higher intensity signifies greater filtration. By following these simple yet detailed steps, you have successfully enabled Night Light on your Android Oreo device. Remember, regular usage of Night Light can significantly decrease digital eyestrain and improve sleep quality since reduced exposure to blue light promotes melatonin production necessary for good sleep. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Keep your device software updated regularly for bug fixes and improvements including Night Light functionality. - Be aware that enabling Night Light may consume slightly more battery life due to increased rendering workload. - Consider using third-party applications offering advanced features like auto-sunrise/sunset timing and manual triggering. oh, Nina! has her doll house all full of Christmas cards. Title: Figure Out What to Donate: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Deciding what to donate can be an overwhelming task due to various factors such as emotional attachment, time constraints, or simply having too many items to sort through. This guide will help you navigate this process with ease by breaking it down into manageable steps. By following these steps, you'll declutter your space while making a positive impact on others. Step 1: Set Clear Goals Determine why you want to donate. Are you clearing out clutter? Downsizing? Making room for new things? Having clear goals will keep you focused throughout the process. Key Tip: Be specific about your objectives. For example, instead of saying "I want to get rid of some clothes," say "I aim to fill two boxes with unwanted clothing." Step 2: Identify Items You No Longer Need Start with one area at a time - perhaps a closet, bookshelf, or garage. Remove everything from that space and place them in categories (e.g., shirts, pants, shoes). This physical separation allows you to see exactly how much you have and makes decision-making easier. Guideline: Use the 'one year rule.' If you haven't used something within the past year, consider letting it go unless it holds significant sentimental value. Step 3: Assess Each Item Individually Pick up each item and ask yourself if you truly need or love it. Remember, just because something was expensive or given as a gift doesn't mean you must hold onto it forever. Think about whether someone else could benefit more from owning it than you currently do. Key Tips: * Avoid rushing decisions; take breaks when needed. * Enlist a friend or family member to provide an objective perspective. Step 4: Research Potential Recipients Look up local charities, thrift stores, shelters, or nonprofits that align with your values and accept the type of items you wish to donate. Websites like CharityNavigator.org can help verify their legitimacy and effectiveness. Step 5: Check Acceptance Policies Each organization has different policies regarding what they accept. Some may refuse damaged goods, certain types of electronics, or furniture without proper fire safety labels. Always call ahead or check online before dropping off your donation. Step 6: Clean and Prepare Your Donations Wash, dry clean, or repair any items as necessary before packing them up. Not only is this courteous, but it also increases the likelihood of your donated goods being utilized rather than discarded. Step 7: Schedule Delivery or Pickup Some organizations offer pickup services, especially for large items, which saves you time and effort. Alternatively, plan a trip to deliver your donations during operating hours. Step 8: Document Your Donations Keep track of all donated items for tax purposes. Most organizations provide receipts upon request. While not everyone chooses to claim these deductions, having records ensures options are available should you choose to do so later. Conclusion: Figuring out what to donate requires mindfulness, research, and preparation. But remember, every item you give away frees up both physical and mental space, ultimately contributing to a less stressed and more fulfilling life. Happy donating! [Eve wants to lie down in small baby's basket] Title: How to Change Your Desktop Wallpaper on Linux Mint Introduction: Changing your desktop wallpaper on Linux Mint is an easy process that allows you to personalize your computing experience. This tutorial will guide you through changing your wallpaper using both the default settings manager and a third-party application called Variety Wallpaper Changer. By following these steps, you can add a touch of personality to your workspace. **Section 1: Using the Default Settings Manager** Step 1: Access System Settings ------------------------------- * Click on the Start Menu (the mint icon at the bottom left corner) * Type "System Settings" into the search bar * Select "System Settings" from the results list This action opens the main configuration panel where you can manage various system aspects. Step 2: Navigate to Backgrounds Section --------------------------------------- Inside the System Settings window, click on the "Backgrounds" category located under the "Appearance" section. The backgrounds preferences pane appears with options to customize your desktop image. Step 3: Choose New Wallpaper --------------------------- Clicking the dropdown menu next to "Wallpapers," you'll see several preinstalled images available by default. You may select one of those or choose to upload your own image file. Key Tips & Guidelines: * To set your own picture as wallpaper, click on the '+' button beside the dropdown menu. Then navigate to the directory containing your chosen image and double-click it. * For optimal display quality, ensure that the resolution of your selected image matches or exceeds your screen resolution. Step 4: Apply Changes -------------------- After selecting your preferred wallpaper, simply close the System Settings window. The new background should now be visible across all workspaces. **Section 2: Utilizing Variety Wallpaper Changer** For users who want more advanced features like automatic wallpaper changes, multiple sources integration, and effects, installing Variety Wallpaper Changer could be beneficial. Here's how to do this: Step 1: Install Flatpak Package Manager ---------------------------------------- Flatpak is required because Variety isn't officially included in Linux Mint repositories yet. Open Terminal and run: ```bash sudo apt update && sudo apt install flatpak ``` Step 2: Add Flathub Repository ------------------------------ Run the command below to add the necessary repository: ```bash flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo ``` Step 3: Install Variety ---------------------- Now you can install Variety via Flatpak: ```bash flatpak install flathub org.varietywallpaper.Variety ``` Step 4: Run Variety ------------------ Launch Variety either from the start menu or typing `variety` in the terminal. Once open, configure its settings according to your preference. Conclusion: By completing this tutorial, you have successfully learned two methods for changing your desktop wallpaper on Linux Mint - one utilizing built-in tools and another leveraging additional software. With these skills, you can tailor your desktop environment to suit your individual tastes! The 1930s was the decade that started on January 1, 1930 and ended on December 31, 1939. Title: How to Fix Dry Cookie Dough Introduction: Dry cookie dough can be frustrating when you're trying to bake delicious cookies. However, there are several ways to fix this common issue. This tutorial will guide you through various methods to revive your dry cookie dough, ensuring that your baking experience is successful and enjoyable. Method 1 - Adding Liquid Ingredients: Step 1: Identify the Issue Determine if your dough is too dry by checking its consistency. If it doesn't hold together or forms cracks when pressed, then it likely needs more moisture. Step 2: Choose an Appropriate Liquid Ingredient Select a liquid ingredient from your recipe such as milk, water, eggs, oil, melted butter, or vanilla extract. Avoid adding too much raw egg at once because it may change the taste and texture of your final product. Step 3: Gradually Mix-in the Liquid Add small amounts (about one teaspoon) of your chosen liquid into the dough while mixing gently until you reach the right consistency. Be careful not to overmix, which could lead to tough cookies. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Start with less liquid than you think you need; you can always add more but cannot take it back out. - Remember that different liquids contribute differently to the overall flavor profile. Use discretion based on what would complement your specific recipe best. Method 2 - Using Fruit Puree: This method works well particularly when making fruity cookies like oatmeal raisin or apricot drops. The fruit puree not only adds moisture but also brings extra flavor. Step 1: Prepare Your Fruit Puree Blend or process your chosen fruit until smooth. Strain if necessary to remove seeds or large chunks. Step 2: Incorporate the Puree Mix the fruit puree into your dry cookie dough just as you would any other liquid. Begin with small increments, stirring thoroughly before deciding whether additional puree is needed. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Opt for mildly flavored fruits unless you want a strong fruit presence in your cookies. - Keep in mind that some fruits contain natural pectins which might affect the final texture of your cookies. Method 3 - Employing Yogurt or Sour Cream: These dairy products offer both creaminess and acidity, enhancing the taste while fixing your dry cookie dough problem. Step 1: Select Your Dairy Product Choose either plain yogurt or sour cream depending on the desired level of tanginess in your finished cookies. Step 2: Blend Carefully Stir in your selected dairy product slowly, starting with tiny portions. Combine gently so as not to activate excessive gluten development leading to hard cookies. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Full-fat versions provide better results due to their higher fat content. - Adjust the quantity according to the size of your batch. Generally, use around two tablespoons per cup of flour. Conclusion: By following these simple yet effective techniques, you should now have successfully rescued your dry cookie dough! Always remember to adjust gradually and avoid overmixing for optimal results. Happy Baking! Very good and it's the button on the radio right? it's going to come sneakin' up and you have to catch it. You could say you could add in all the things that... = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Soderstrom/Joe/000801.cha = = = Determining the Polarity of Magnets: A Comprehensive Guide Magnets are fascinating objects that have two poles - north and south. Understanding the concept of magnetic polarity is crucial for various applications such as science experiments, engineering projects, or even just for fun. This guide will walk you through the process of determining the polarity of magnets in a clear and detailed manner. **Step 1: Gather Your Materials** You will need: - Two magnets (the more identical they are, the better) - A non-magnetic surface (like a tabletop or whiteboard) Having similar sized and shaped magnets makes it easier to observe their behavior accurately. A non-magnetic surface ensures that there's no interference from other magnetic fields during your investigation. **Step 2: Familiarize Yourself with Magnet Basics** Before starting, understand that like poles repel each other while opposite poles attract. If you push the same ends together, they'll push away; if you bring different ends close, they'll pull towards one another. **Step 3: Prepare Your Workspace** Place your non-magnetic surface on a stable, flat area where you can work comfortably without disturbance. This sets up an ideal environment for conducting your experiment. **Step 4: Position the First Magnet** Take one magnet and place it firmly on the non-magnetic surface. We'll call this Magnet A. Make sure its position remains unchanged throughout your testing so that results aren't skewed. **Step 5: Test With the Second Magnet** Now take the second magnet, referred to as Magnet B, and hold it above Magnet A. Slowly move Magnet B closer to Magnet A while paying attention to their interaction. Remember, if Magnet B jumps or moves towards Magnet A, then they're attracted to each other, meaning they have opposite polarities. Conversely, if Magnet B tries to move away or 'repels', then they share the same polarity. **Key Tip:** Perform these tests slowly and carefully. Quick movements might cause unexpected results due to momentum rather than magnetic force. **Step 6: Identify North and South Poles** Based on your observations, label the poles accordingly. The pole of Magnet B that was attracted to Magnet A becomes the 'north' pole, while the one that repelled becomes the 'south' pole. Similarly, identify the north and south poles of Magnet A by repeating Step 5 but using Magnet B as the reference point. **Guideline:** Always double-check your identifications since mistakes can lead to incorrect conclusions about magnetic polarities. **Step 7: Record Your Findings** Document your findings meticulously, noting down which end of each magnet corresponds to the north and south poles. Keeping records allows you to revisit your conclusions later and provides valuable data for future references. By following these steps, you should now be able to determine the polarity of any pair of magnets! Just remember to always handle magnets safely, especially when dealing with strong ones, as they can pinch skin or damage certain electronic devices. Customizing your avatar on PlayStation 4 (PS4) is an excellent way to personalize your gaming experience and express yourself. This tutorial will guide you through the process of changing your avatar on your PS4, providing in-depth explanations for each step and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Navigate to the Function area** 1. Turn on your PS4 and sign in to your account. 2. From the home screen, locate and select the 'Function' area, which can be found at the far right side of the menu. The Function area contains various settings and options related to your user profile and system configuration. **Step 2: Access User Profile Settings** 1. Scroll down until you find the 'Settings' icon, represented by a toolbox. Select this option to proceed. 2. Within the Settings menu, scroll down to 'Users,' then choose 'Accounts Management.' 3. Next, select 'Profile' from the Accounts Management submenu to access your user profile information and customization options. **Step 3: Edit your Online ID (Optional)** 1. If you wish to change your Online ID, select 'Online ID' within the Profile menu. Note that there may be limitations when changing your Online ID, including possible compatibility issues with certain games. Always check Sony's official support page before making changes to ensure minimal disruption to your gaming experience. 2. Follow the prompts to enter your new Online ID and confirm any necessary payment details if required. Keep in mind that some users might encounter charges for specific Online ID changes depending on their region or membership status. **Step 4: Update your Avatar Image** 1. Return to the main Profile menu after editing your Online ID, if applicable. 2. Locate and select 'Avatars' to view available avatar images provided by Sony. You can browse different categories such as characters, animals, objects, and more to find one that suits your preferences. 3. Once you have selected an image, press the 'X' button to set it as your default avatar. Alternatively, you can also preview additional poses for your chosen avatar by pressing left or right on the directional pad while highlighting the desired image. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Be cautious about using explicit content as your avatar image, as Sony has strict community guidelines regarding offensive material. Violating these rules could result in penalties, including temporary or permanent bans from online services. * Remember that changing your avatar does not affect your saved data, game progress, or other aspects of your user profile. It only alters your visual representation within the PS4 interface and online communities. * Consider choosing an avatar that reflects your personality or interests, as this can help create a positive first impression when interacting with other gamers. * While browsing through available avatars, pay attention to detail and color schemes, ensuring they are clear and visible against different background colors used throughout the PS4 interface. * To further enhance your personalized gaming experience, explore other customizable features offered by your PS4, such as setting up custom themes, configuring controller layouts, or adjusting parental controls to suit your needs. yeah, at the daycare she's awesome, and she has like a math curriculum for these kids you know zero to three years old. You can twist one end of the ribbon before gluing it to the other end. If you did this correctly, you would have created a shape called a M bius band! It's a fascinating object with some unique properties. Now, let me tell you about another way to describe the M bius band using something called "quotients." Don't worry - we won't dive into complex math here. Instead, think of it as dividing things into groups based on certain rules. Imagine having a cylindrical tube made of paper. Now, imagine you could squish this tube flat while allowing both ends to match up perfectly after twisting them once. This results in a shape very similar to our twisted paper ribbon - a M bius band! In fact, these two shapes are considered the same when talking about quotients because we divided the cylinder according to specific rules. But why does any of this matter? Well, sometimes mathematicians prefer working with one type of shape over another depending on what they want to study or prove. So, even though these seem like different shapes, understanding their equivalence allows us to connect ideas between various branches of mathematics seamlessly. Finally, going back to your original question, since both the twisted paper ribbon and the squished cylinder represent the same idea, they share many characteristics, including being non-orientable surfaces. What are Black Holes Anyway? Black holes are not really holes at all. They are the opposite of empty! Black holes have the most matter stuffed into the least space of any objects in the universe. Because they are so compact, they have very strong gravity. Here on Earth, gravity is what makes things fall down, rather than just float away, when you let go of them. Gravity is what you are measuring when you step on a scale to weigh yourself. Your weight is the amount of force that Earth's gravity exerts on you. The more matter your body contains, the more you weigh. Likewise, the more matter an object has, the stronger its gravity. The gravity of a black hole is so strong that not even light can escape. Even if a bright star is shining right next to a black hole, you cannot see the black hole. Instead of reflecting the light as other objects do, the black hole just swallows the starlight forever. Any matter that gets too close to a black hole gets swallowed up as well. There are at least two kinds of black holes One kind is called a stellar-mass black hole. You can think of it as a "one-big-star" black hole. This type of black hole forms when a big star burns up all its fuel and explodes (called a supernova). Then what's left collapses into a super-compact object-a black hole. Stars must contain quite a bit more matter than our Sun for this to happen. So our Sun, and most stars, will never become black holes. Stellar-mass black holes are only a few tens of kilometers across-maybe about 40 miles. Just imagine. Our Sun is so huge that about one million Earths would fit inside it. A star with enough matter to become a black hole contains maybe 10 times as much matter as the Sun. Now imagine a star with that much matter, shrinking into a space no farther across than the distance you can drive a car in less than one hour! Another kind of black hole is called a supermassive black hole. You can think of this type as a "million-big-star" black hole, because it contains as much matter as one million to 100 million Suns! Astronomers think that supermassive black holes are lurking at the centers of galaxies, including our own Milky Way galaxy. They don't know yet how these humongous black holes are formed. Learning More About Black Holes Scientists really want to learn more about black holes and other strange and massive objects in the Universe. One space mission that is helping them do just that is a space telescope called XMM-Newton. It was launched into Earth orbit in 1999 by NASA and the European Space Agency. It observes the universe in high-energy x-rays, a type of light that we can't see with our eyes. Matter, such as gas and dust particles, near black holes puts out x-rays as it swirls around at light speed just before the black hole swallows it up. By observing these x-rays, XMM can help scientists understand the black hole. Here you want the book it's kind of like it? What are you going to do with them? What did you do with your shirt Ross? But what discoveries did we actually make? ENTER MARS! A planet that could have been exactly like ours! Amazing facts revealed! Mars is the only planet whose surface can be seen in detail from the Earth. It is reddish in color, and was named after the bloody red God of war of the ancient Romans. Mars is the fourth closest planet to the sun. The diameter of Mars is 4,200 miles, a little over half that of the Earth. Mercury is the only planet smaller than Mars. Mars Facts You Might Not Know About Mars is red because it is rusty. There is a lot of iron in the soil, and the air on Mars has made it turn red-just like rusty iron on Earth. One of Mars' moons, Phobos, is moving closer and closer to Mars. Scientists think that one day it will crash into Mars. Mars has the tallest Volcano in the Solar System named Olympus Mons and it is 15 miles high which is three times the height of Mount Everest. Like Earth, the poles of Mars are covered in ice. The ice becomes thicker in the winter. Mars orbits the sun every 687 Earth days. The Martian "day" is about half a hour longer than Earth. At its brightest, Mars outshines every other planet apart from Venus. The thin atmosphere of Mars is made of mostly carbon dioxide. A hundred pound man would weight 38 pounds on Mars. In 1996 NASA, while studying the ALH 84001 meteorite of Martian origin found in Antarctica in 1984, announced that fossilized micro-organisms from Mars might be present in it. Valleys and Canyons on Mars suggest that the planet once had large amounts of surface water. In winter, nighttime temperatures on Mars can drop as low as -191 F. How much would you weigh on Mars? If you weighed 70 pounds (32 kg) on the Earth, you would weigh about 27 pounds (12 kg) on Mars. Mars excites scientists because its mild temperament is more like the Earth's than any of the other planets. Evidence suggests that Mars once had rivers, streams, lakes, and even an ocean. As Mars' atmosphere slowly depleted into outer space, the surface water began to permanently evaporate. Today the only water on Mars in either frozen in the polar caps or underground. You may sometimes hear Mars referred to as the "Red Planet." This is because the surface of Mars is red. If you stood on the surface of Mars, you would see red dirt and rocks everywhere. Title: How to Build a Cardboard House Introduction: Making a cardboard house can be an enjoyable and creative activity for both children and adults alike. It's eco-friendly, budget-friendly, and encourages imagination. This project is also great for developing fine motor skills, spatial awareness, and problem-solving abilities. Here we will guide you through building your own cardboard house with simple materials and tools that are easy to find. Materials Needed: 1. Large pieces of sturdy cardboard (at least three large boxes) 2. Ruler or measuring tape 3. Pencil 4. Scissors or box cutter knife 5. Packing tape 6. Glue gun and glue sticks 7. Decorative items such as markers, paint, stickers etc. Step 1: Planning Your Design Decide on the size and shape of your cardboard house. Sketch out a rough design on paper including measurements, which will help ensure accuracy during construction. Consider factors like where windows should go, if any, and what height would work best. Key tip: Remember, bigger doesn't always mean better; keep structural integrity in mind when designing. Step 2: Cutting Out the Base Using your measured sketch from Step 1, draw two identical rectangles onto your largest piece of cardboard. These will form the front and back walls of your house. Make sure they're wide enough for doors or windows if included in your design. Then, carefully cut them out using scissors or a box cutter knife. Step 3: Creating Sidewalls From another box, draw and cut four smaller rectangular panels - these will serve as the sides and roof supports. Two must match the width of your base while the other two need to be slightly shorter than the height of your door frame. Step 4: Assembling the Structure Tape one pair of matching sidewall panels together along their length edges creating an L shape. Repeat this process with the second set. Now attach these L shapes to the inside corners of your base structure forming a square standing upright. Use packing tape to secure all joints firmly. Step 5: Adding Roof Panels Take the remaining two cardboard strips and place them horizontally across the top edge of the newly formed 'box'. They should overlap at the center point above the peak of your doorway. Securely affix them with packing tape. Step 6: Making Windows & Doorways Cut out openings for doors and windows according to your initial plan. For added stability around larger openings, consider reinforcing the area by attaching extra layers of cardboard behind the opening before cutting it out completely. Step 7: Sealing Edges To prevent collapses or injuries, seal off any sharp edges or gaps with either more tape or hot glue. Be careful with hot glue though, especially near kids! Step 8: Decoration Time Now comes the fun part - decorating! Unleash creativity with paints, colored pens, stickers, fabric scraps...the possibilities are endless. Let your inner artist shine! Conclusion: Your cardboard house is now complete! With patience, precision, and some artistic flair, you've created a unique space filled with hours of imaginative play potential. Enjoy watching your creation come alive in whatever storyline you choose! It is just like the stuff at home. No I'm rinsing out the coffeepot so I can give Pop some more coffee. Today we're going to learn about something cool that happens when two sets of parallel lines get interrupted by another line. This other line is called a "transversal." Have you ever seen train tracks? They go on forever in the same direction, right? Now imagine if there was a road crossing those tracks - that road would be our transversal! When this special road crosses the two sets of train tracks (or any pair of parallel lines), it creates some neat angles outside of the lines. We call these "exterior angles," because they're found outside the actual lines themselves. Let me show you with a diagram: [Insert Diagram Showing Two Sets Of Parallel Lines With A Transversal Intersecting And Creating Four Exterior Angles] There are four exterior angles made here - let's label them together! APX is one, XPB is another, then comes DQY, followed by YQC. That makes up all four of the unique exterior angles created by the transversal. Now, I bet you're wondering why learning about these weird-looking angles even matters. Well, understanding these will actually make solving mysteries like this easier: [Show Picture Of An Intersection Between Three Streets Forming Six Visible Angles] Look closely; do you see anything interesting? By using our new knowledge about exterior angles, we could figure out if these streets have any hidden connections, just like secret passages between rooms! It turns out that if two pairs of opposite angles along a triangle formed by three intersections are equal, then those corners must belong to the same set of parallel lines! Isn't that amazing? So remember, whenever you encounter situations where parallel lines meet a transversal, think back to these awesome exterior angles we learned today! He has a book very similar to this one. Many big rivers in the surrounding countries drain into the Baltic Sea. The Baltic Sea is connected to the ocean through the narrow and shallow Danish straits and belts. For this reason, the water has a low amount of salt, so the northern parts of the sea freeze over in the winter. The original IPv4 only supported 254 networks, so in 1981 the Internet addressing specification was changed to a classful network architecture. Classful network design allowed for a larger number of individual networks. The first three bits of an IP address determined its "class". Three classes ("A", "B", and "C") were defined for normal computer communication (Unicast). The size of the network ID was based on the class of the IP address. Each class used more octets for the network ID, making the host ID smaller and reducing the number of possible hosts. And he'll do that with um bears too. Give baby pat pats to go nigh. Arlo you height come over here so I can XXX. Certain currencies are said to be "pegged" or "fixed" to something, usually another currency. A fixed or pegged currency is one that has a constant value compared to what it is pegged to. For example, the Cape Verdian escudo is pegged to the Euro. If the value of the Euro goes up 1% compared to another form of currency, the value of the escudo also goes up 1% compared to that same currency. Why are you getting it all over your face? Title: How to Give Your Chinchilla a Dust Bath Introduction: Chinchillas are rodents native to South America's Andes mountains. They have thick fur that requires regular dust baths to stay clean and healthy. This article will guide you through providing your chinchilla with an appropriate dust bath experience. Materials Needed: 1. Chinchilla-safe dust (not play sand or human bath products) 2. Appropriate container for the dust bath 3. Space where the dust bath can be contained and cleaned up easily Step 1: Purchase Chinchilla-Safe Dust Do not use play sand, bird sand, or any other type of sand or household product as these can harm your pet. Instead, buy commercial chinchilla dust which is available at most pet stores. It should be made from natural volcanic rock finely ground into a soft powder consistency. Different brands may vary slightly; choose one labeled specifically for chinchillas. Step 2: Choose an Ideal Container The container must be large enough for your chinchilla to roll around in but shallow so they cannot ingest the dust. Avoid deep containers like fishbowls. Many people use baking pans, cat litter boxes, or plastic storage bins. Make sure there are high sides to prevent the dust from spreading too much during the bath. Step 3: Prepare the Bathing Area Place the chosen container filled about two inches deep with the chinchilla dust in a space where cleanup won't be difficult. Spread newspapers, old towels, or a washable mat underneath to catch excess dust. Never give a chinchilla a dust bath in its cage to avoid making it dirty. Step 4: Introduce Your Chinchilla to the Dust Bath Let your chinchilla explore the new setup freely. Most chinchillas take quickly to dust baths, enjoying the feeling of rolling in the dust. If yours seems hesitant, try placing some of their droppings in the dust to encourage them. Also, ensure no predators are nearby as this could stress out your chinchilla. Step 5: Observe Your Pet During Its Bath Supervise your chinchilla while it takes its bath to ensure safety. Some animals might eat the dust, which can lead to digestive problems. Stop the bath if you notice such behavior. Generally, limit the dust bath time to around 10-15 minutes per session, once or twice a week. Overbathing can dry out their skin. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Always provide fresh dust for every bath. Stale dust can harbor bacteria. - After the bath, gently brush your chinchilla with a soft bristle brush to remove loose hair and extra dust. - Keep dust away from electronics and ventilation systems to avoid damage or health risks. - Consult with a vet if your chinchilla shows signs of skin irritation after a dust bath. Conclusion: Providing regular dust baths is crucial for maintaining your chinchilla's coat and overall wellbeing. With patience, practice, and adherence to these steps, both you and your beloved pet will enjoy this important aspect of chinchilla care. I think that's a big swordfish. Title: How to Perform the Hand Motions to the "Na Na Na" Song The "Na Na Na" song, also known as "Hey Jude" by The Beatles, is an iconic tune with memorable hand motions that have been performed at countless events and gatherings. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to do the hand motions to this classic track. By following these steps, you'll be able to engage your audience and create a fun, interactive experience during any performance or karaoke session. **Step 1:** Familiarize Yourself with the Song Listen to the "Na Na Na" section of "Hey Jude" multiple times to ensure you know when the hand motion segment occurs. Understanding the rhythm and flow of the music is crucial before attempting the accompanying hand movements. Key Tip: Pay attention to the build-up towards the "Na Na Na" part, which typically starts after the second chorus. **Step 2:** Position Your Hands Correctly Place both hands, palms facing outwards, at chest level with fingers slightly spread apart. Keep your elbows bent at around a 90-degree angle. Maintain this position throughout most of the hand motion sequence to establish consistency and enable easier synchronization with the song. ![Hand positioning]( " I be Ya know what, I believe you have a very big um. By: Melinda Hardin Rationale: For children to become skilled readers they must understand that correspondences appear differently in different words. Also children need to know the difference between long and short vowels. The have to understand that these correspondences are spelled and pronounced differently. In this lesson we will review the i=/i/ correspondence and introduce the correspondence i_e=/I/. The students will be introduced to the correspondence through spelling words with letterboxes and later reading them. Also to better chk their understanding of the new correspondence we will have them read pseudo words. White board and marker Picture of ice cream, with i_e in it Sign with "Ivy's ice cream is icy" with another ice cream picture Teacher letterbox and letters Letterboxes for each student Letters for each students: b, c, d, e, f, g, i, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, w Individual copies for each student of the book Di and the Mice (Phonics Readers Long Vowels, Book 6 -- Long i). Publisher: Educational Insights (1990) Note cards with pseudo words (such words can include: pim, fime, scrime, rin, tine) 1.Now that we have already learned all of our short vowels, we are going to move on to long vowels. To begin introduce the lesson by reviewing i=/i/. Can everyone remember what I says when it's all by itself in a word? "Icky Sticky"?. Since we know that when we see I alone in a word it says /i/. Today we are going to find out what sound we make when there is an i, a consonant, and then an e at the end of the word. (Write i_e on the board). When we see i_e in a word, the i says its name /I/ and the e is silent. Okay now we need to look at our Ice Cream poster. Whoever likes ice cream raise their hand. Great! So whenever we see i_e in our reading, we are going to say /I/ and lick our Ice Cream cones. Now let's all try it, /I/. 2.Now we are going to say this tongue twister together. ) Make sure the students can see the poster) "Ivy's ice cream is icy." Who can hear the /I/ in our tongue twister? Good. Every time we hear the /I/ in our tongue twister we are going to get our ice cream cones and act like we are licking them. Let's try: "IIIIIIIIvy'sIIIIIce cream is IIIIcy." Great job! 3.Everyone look at this word on the board. (Write the word pine on the board) Who can come up here and underline the i_e in this word? (Have one of the students come up to the board and underline the i and e). Whenever we see the i_e we need to remember that is a signal that the i
